morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 走/让步 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Old French concession (14c.) or directly from Latin concessionem (nominative concessio) "an allowing, conceding," noun of action from past participle stem of concedere (see concede).

meanings.definitionCn: 正确无误的；准确的，精确的

etymology.story: 1610s, "done with care," from Latin accuratus "prepared with care, exact, elaborate," past participle of accurare "take care of," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + curare "take care of" (see cure (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 维持；继续；维修；主张；供养

etymology.story: mid-13c., "to practice habitually," from Anglo-French meintenir (Old French maintenir, 12c.) "keep (a wife), sustain; persevere in, practice continually," from Latin manu tenere "hold in the hand," from manu, ablative of manus "hand" (see manual) + tenere "to hold" (see tenet).

Old English sciftan, scyftan "arrange, place, order," also "divide, partition; distribute, allot, share," from Proto-Germanic *skiftan (source also of Old Norse skipta "to divide, change, separate," Old Frisian skifta "to decide, determine, test," Dutch schiften "to divide, turn," German schichten "to classify," Schicht "shift").
mid-14c., "business transaction or agreement," also "that which is acquired by bargaining," from Old French bargaine, from bargaignier (see bargain (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 专业的；职业的；职业性的

morphology.mnemonic: declare openly + 状态/行为/过程 + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 选举的，有选举权的；可任意选择的

etymology.story: early 15c., "voluntary," from Late Latin electivus, from elect-, past participle stem of eligere "to pick out, choose" (see election).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French acusacion or directly from Latin accusationem (nominative accusatio), noun of action from past participle stem of accusare (see accuse)..

1844, from French protéine, coined 1838 by Dutch chemist Gerhard Johan Mulder (1802-1880), perhaps on suggestion of Berzelius, from Greek proteios "the first quality," from protos "first" (see proto-) + -ine (2).

morphology.mnemonic: 年 + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 独占的;排他的;孤高的;唯一的;高级的

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 关闭 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "so as to exclude;" 1560s, "that excludes," from Medieval Latin exclusivus, from exclus-, past participle stem of excludere (see exclude).

"simple earthen or glass cylindrical vessel," early 15c., possibly from Old French jarre "liquid measure smaller than a barrel" (12c.), perhaps from Provençal jarra, from Arabic jarrah "earthen water vessel, ewer" (whence also Spanish jarra, Italian giarra), which is from Persian jarrah "a jar, earthen water-vessel.".
late 12c., from Old French poverte "poverty, misery, wretched condition" (Modern French pauvreté), from Latin paupertatem (nominative paupertas) "poverty," from pauper "poor" (see poor (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 凭借，手段；度假胜地；常去之地
求助，诉诸；常去；采取某手段或方法

morphology.mnemonic: the good side + 状态/性质

etymology.story: Old English godnes "goodness, virtue, kindliness;" see good (adj.) + -ness.
late 13c., present participle of lightnen "make bright," extended form of Old English lihting, from leht (see light (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 页边空白;边缘;余地;幅度
保持；依然；留下；剩余；逗留；残存

etymology.story: "those left over or surviving," mid-15c., from Middle French remain, back-formation from Old French remanoir, remaindre, or else formed in Middle English from remain (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 精炼,精制,提纯;使文雅,使高尚
增加；爬上；使骑上马；安装，架置；镶嵌，嵌入；准备上演；成立（军队等）
山峰；底座；乘骑用马；攀，登；运载工具；底座
要求， 请求， 需要； 查问
要求， 请求， 需要

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + 命令/委托

etymology.story: late 13c., "a question," from Old French demande (see demand (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 秘密，谜；神秘，神秘的事物；推理小说，推理剧；常作 mysteries 秘技，秘诀

etymology.story: 1670s as a philosophical term (see atomistic); scientific sense dates from 1811, from atom + -ic.
1620s, from Latin depositum, from deponere (see deposit (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 腐烂的；堕落的；恶臭的；虚弱的；极坏的

etymology.story: also skilful, early 14c., from skill + -ful.

meanings.definitionCn: 房屋；住房供给；[机] 外壳；遮盖物；机器等的防护外壳或外罩
魔鬼；撒旦；家伙；恶棍；淘气鬼；冒失鬼
虐待，折磨；（用扯碎机）扯碎；（替作家，律师等）做助手；抹辣味料烤制或煎煮

etymology.story: Old English deofol "evil spirit, a devil, the devil, false god, diabolical person," from Late Latin diabolus (also the source of Italian diavolo, French diable, Spanish diablo; German Teufel is Old High German tiufal, from Latin via Gothic diabaulus).

meanings.definitionCn: 推荐， 介绍；劝告， 建议

etymology.story: late 14c., "praise, present as worthy," from Medieval Latin recommendare, from Latin re-, here probably an intensive prefix, or else from a sense now obscure (see re-), + commendare "commit to one's care, commend" (see commend).
mid-15c., acqueren, from Old French aquerre "acquire, gain, earn, procure," from Vulgar Latin *acquaerere, corresponding to Latin acquirere "to seek in addition to" (see acquisition).

meanings.definitionCn: 富饶的，肥沃的；能生育的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "bearing or producing abundantly," from Middle French fertil (15c.) and directly from Latin fertilis "bearing in abundance, fruitful, productive," from ferre "to bear" (see infer).
"self-propelled motor vehicle," 1895, from French automobile, short for véhicule automobile (see automobile (adj.)).
late 14c., from Latin notionem (nominative notio) "concept, conception, idea, notice," noun of action from past participle stem of noscere "come to know" (see know).

morphology.mnemonic: different in character or quality. + ity

etymology.story: mid-14c., "quality of being diverse," mostly in a neutral sense, from Old French diversité (12c.) "difference, diversity, unique feature, oddness:" also "wickedness, perversity," from Latin diversitatem (nominative diversitas) "contrariety, contradiction, disagreement;" also, as a secondary sense, "difference, diversity," from diversus "turned different ways" (in Late Latin "various"), past participle of divertere (see divert).

1861, from French hélicoptère "device for enabling airplanes to rise perpendicularly," thus "flying machine propelled by screws." From Greek helix (genitive helikos) "spiral" (see helix) + pteron "wing" (see ptero-).
1856, "something that pulls," from Modern Latin tractor "that which draws," agent noun from past participle stem of Latin trahere "to pull, draw" (see tract (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 优美,文雅;恩惠,恩泽;宽限,缓刑;感恩祷告
广告， 公告； 广告活动， 宣传

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + ize + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: early 15c., "written statement calling attention to" something, "public notice" (of anything, but often of a sale); from Middle French avertissement, from stem of avertir (see advertise).

meanings.definitionCn: 熟的，成熟的；时机成熟的

etymology.story: Old English ripe "ready for reaping, fit for eating, mature," from West Germanic *ripijaz (source also of Old Saxon ripi, Middle Dutch ripe, Dutch rijp, Old High German rifi, German reif); related to Old English repan "to reap" (see reap).
"quality ascribed to someone," late 14c., from Latin attributum "anything attributed," noun use of neuter of attributus (see attribute (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 机制；原理，途径；进程；机械装置；技巧

morphology.mnemonic: 机械 + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: 1660s, from Modern Latin mechanismus, from Greek mekhane "machine, instrument" (see machine (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 察觉， 发觉； 侦查， 探测

etymology.story: early 15c., "exposure, accusation," from Latin detectionem (nominative detectio) "an uncovering," noun of action from past participle stem of detegere (see detect)..
mid-15c., from Old French triumpher (13c.), from Latin triumphare, from triumphus (see triumph (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 恶习；缺点；[机] 老虎钳；卖淫
惊骇；惨状；极端厌恶；令人恐怖的事物

etymology.story: early 14c., "feeling of disgust;" late 14c., "emotion of horror or dread," also "thing which excites horror," from Old French horror (12c., Modern French horreur) and directly from Latin horror "dread, veneration, religious awe," a figurative use, literally "a shaking, trembling (as with cold or fear), shudder, chill," from horrere "to bristle with fear, shudder," from PIE root *ghers- "to bristle" (source also of Sanskrit harsate "bristles," Avestan zarshayamna- "ruffling one's feathers," Latin eris (genitive) "hedgehog," Welsh garw "rough").

meanings.definitionCn: 污染；使中毒，放毒于；败坏；阻碍
毒药，毒物；酒；有毒害的事物；[助剂] 抑制剂

etymology.story: "to give poison to; kill with poison," c.
1650s, also impower, from assimilated form of en- (1) + power (n.).
late 14c., "behave in a dissolute manner, engage in loose revelry," from Old French rioter "chatter, dispute, quarrel," from riote (see riot (n.)).
1590s, "awaken" (transitive), from a- (1) "on" + rouse.
Old English nistan "to build nests," from Proto-Germanic *nistijanan, from the source of nest (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 旅行，在……旅游；在……作巡回演出

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French calmer or from calm (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 令人厌烦的， 乏味的， 无聊的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "action of piercing," from bore (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 经典的；古典的，传统的；最优秀的
名著；经典著作；大艺术家

morphology.mnemonic: own + er + 身份/状态/技能

etymology.story: 1580s, from owner + -ship.
"science of versification," 1760, from Greek he metrike "prosody," plural of metron "meter, a verse; that by which anything is measured; measure, length, size, limit, proportion" (see meter (n.2))..

meanings.definitionCn: 绝对的，完全的；确实的，肯定的

etymology.story: late 14c., "unrestricted; complete, perfect;" also "not relative to something else" (mid-15c.), from Middle French absolut (14c., Old French asolu, Modern French absolu), from Latin absolutus, past participle of absolvere "to set free, make separate" (see absolve).
early 13c., descriven, from Old French descrivre, descrire (13c.), from Latin describere "to write down, copy; sketch, represent" (see description).

Old English sucan "to suck," from a Proto-Germanic word of imitative origin (cognates: Old Saxon, Old High German sugan, Old Norse suga, Danish suge, Swedish suga, Middle Dutch sughen, Dutch zuigen, German saugen "to suck"), possibly from the same source as Latin sugere "to suck," succus "juice, sap;" Old Irish sugim, Welsh sugno "to suck;" see sup (v.2).
late 14c., "immediately, at this time," from present (adj.) + -ly (2).
late 14c., "happy, prosperous," from wealth + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 小地毯；毛皮地毯；男子假发

etymology.story: 1550s, "coarse fabric," of Scandinavian origin; compare Norwegian dialectal rugga "coarse coverlet," from Old Norse rogg "shaggy tuft," from Proto-Germanic *rawwa-, perhaps related to rag (n.) and rough (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 奏爵士乐，跳爵士舞；游荡
管理；行政；实施；行政机构

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 小/突出 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-14c., "act of giving or dispensing;" late 14c., "management, act of administering," from Latin administrationem (nominative administratio) "aid, help, cooperation; direction, management," noun of action from past participle stem of administrare (see administer).

meanings.definitionCn: 偶然的；临时的；特殊场合的

etymology.story: late 14c., "occurring now and then," from occasion (n.) + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 夸口说，自吹自擂说；以有…而自豪
自夸；值得夸耀的事物，引以为荣的事物

etymology.story: early 14c., "to brag, speak arrogantly;" from the same source as boast (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 经营者；操作员；话务员；行家

morphology.mnemonic: 工作 + 使…/具有…的 + …者/…器

etymology.story: 1590s, "one who performs mechanical or surgical operations," agent noun from operate (v.) or from Late Latin operator.
early 14c., "a quarrel, dispute, disagreement," from Old French debat; see debate (v.).
1928, from space (n.) + craft (n.)..
1520s, "act of supplying or providing," from Middle French fourniture "a supply; act of furnishing," from Old French forneture (13c.), from fornir "to furnish" (see furnish).

meanings.definitionCn: 喷射，喷嘴；喷气式飞机；黑玉
有帮助的， 有益的， 有用的

etymology.story: late 14c., from help (n.) + -ful.
1852, "one numerical statistic," see statistics.

meanings.definitionCn: 吸引人的，引人注意的；漂亮的，迷人的

etymology.story: late 14c., "absorptive," from Middle French attractif (14c.), from attract-, past participle stem of attrahere (see attract).
1540s, "noble, magnificent" (of buildings, etc.), from Latin superbus "grand, proud, splendid; haughty, vain, insolent," from super "above, over" (see super-).

meanings.definitionCn: 浇铸， 造型； 塑造， 形成

etymology.story: 1720, "work done by an engineer," from engineer (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 有意义的;重大的,重要的

morphology.mnemonic: 标记 + 使…化/造成 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: 1570s, "having a meaning," from Latin significantem (nominative significans, present participle of significare "make known, indicate" (see signify).

meanings.definitionCn: 大胆的，英勇的；黑体的；厚颜无耻的；险峻的

etymology.story: Old English beald (West Saxon), bald (Anglian) "bold, brave, confident, strong," from Proto-Germanic *balthaz (source also of Old High German bald "bold, swift," in names such as Archibald, Leopold, Theobald; Gothic balþei "boldness;" Old Norse ballr "frightful, dangerous"), perhaps from PIE *bhol-to- suffixed form of *bhel- (2) "to blow, swell" (see bole).

Old English bledan "to let blood," in Middle English and after, "to let blood from surgically;" also "to emit blood," from Proto-Germanic *blodjan "emit blood" (source also of Old Norse blæða, German bluten), from *bhlo-to- "swell, gush, spurt" (see blood (n.)).
1590s, "to make a catalogue;" see catalogue (n.).
mid-14c., "what in the world," emphatic of what, with ever.

root.meaning: a fold in a length of cloth

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + a fold in a length of cloth

etymology.story: late 14c., "detriment, harm;" early 15c.

1610s, "the university building in Alexandria," from Latin museum "library, study," from Greek mouseion "place of study, library or museum, school of art or poetry," originally "a seat or shrine of the Muses," from Mousa "Muse" (see muse (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 螺栓，螺钉；闪电，雷电；门闩；弩箭;（布的）一匹，一卷
筛选；囫囵吞下；（把门、窗等）闩上；突然说出，脱口说出
突然地；像箭似地；直立地

etymology.story: Old English streng "line, cord, thread, string of a bow or harp," in plural "tackle, rigging; lineage, race," from Proto-Germanic *strangiz (source also of Old Norse strengr, Danish streng, Middle Dutch strenge, Dutch streng, Old High German strang, German Strang "rope, cord"), from *strang- "taut, stiff," from PIE root *strenk- "tight, narrow." Gradually restricted by early Middle English to lines that are smaller than a rope.

meanings.definitionCn: 进口,输入,(pl.)进口商品;要旨,含意
占,占用;占据,占领;使忙碌,使从事

etymology.story: mid-14c., "to take possession of," also "to take up space or time, employ (someone)," irregularly borrowed from Old French occuper "occupy (a person or place), hold, seize" (13c.) or directly from Latin occupare "take over, seize, take into possession, possess, occupy," from ob "over" (see ob-) + intensive form of capere "to grasp, seize" (see capable).

late 14c., "to place (oneself) under the control of another, to yield oneself," from Latin submittere "to yield, lower, let down, put under, reduce," from sub "under" (see sub-) + mittere "let go, send" (see mission).
mid-15c., earliest use was for bees and wasps, probably echoic of humming.

meanings.definitionCn: 缺乏，不存在；缺席，不在；缺席的时间

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French absence (14c.), from Latin absentia, noun of state from absentem (nominative absens), present participle of abesse "be away from, be absent," from ab- "away" (see ab-) + esse "to be" (see essence)..

meanings.definitionCn: 引起的注意(或兴趣等),吸引；引起；激起

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin attractus, past participle of attrahere "to draw, pull; to attract," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + trahere "draw" (see tract (n.1)).

morphology.mnemonic: to supervise for publication + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "version, translation, a form of a literary work;" 1550s, "act of publishing," from French édition or directly from Latin editionem (nominative editio) "a bringing forth, producing," also "a statement, account," from past participle stem of edere "bring forth, produce," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + -dere, comb.

meanings.definitionCn: 简单,简易;朴素;直率,单纯

etymology.story: late 14c., "singleness of nature, unity, indivisibility; immutability," from Old French simplicite (12c., Modern French simplicité), from Latin simplicitatem (nominative simplicitas) "state of being simple, frankness, openness, artlessness, candor, directness," from simplex (genitive simplicis) "simple" (see simplex).
1640s, from caution + -ous.

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + seem, have a certain appearance

etymology.story: early 15c., disaperen, from dis- "do the opposite of" + appear.

meanings.definitionCn: 最高点， 峰顶， 极点；峰会

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 走/让步 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1640s, "act of receding, a going back," from French récession "a going backward, a withdrawing," and directly from Latin  recessionem (nominative recessio) "a going back," noun of action from past participle stem of recedere (see recede).

meanings.definitionCn: 结论，推论；结尾；缔结，议定

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 关闭 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "deduction or conclusion reached by reasoning," from Old French conclusion "conclusion, result, outcome," from Latin conclusionem (nominative conclusio), noun of action from past participle stem of concludere (see conclude).
1843, "compositor," from type (n.) + -ist.
late 14c., from Old French durable (11c.), from Latin durabilis "lasting, permanent," from durare "to last, harden" (see endure).

meanings.definitionCn: [数] 负的；消极的；否定的；阴性的

morphology.mnemonic: 否定 + 使…/具有…的 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 14c., "a prohibition; absence, nonexistence; opposite," from Old French negatif and directly from Latin negativus (see negative (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 同盟国；伙伴；同盟者；助手
声明；陈述，叙述；报表，清单

etymology.story: 1775, "what is stated," from state (v.) + -ment.

meanings.definitionCn: 从此， 今后； 因此

etymology.story: "(away) from here," late 13c., hennes, with adverbial genitive -s + Old English heonan "away, hence," from West Germanic *hin- (source also of Old Saxon hinan, Old High German hinnan, German hinnen), from PIE *ki-, variant of root *ko- "this," the stem of the demonstrative pronoun (see here).

morphology.mnemonic: memory记忆 + 具有…特征/名词化 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: late 14c., "fame, renown, reputation," also "commemorative gesture, monument, or rite;" in general, "something by which the memory of a person, thing, or event is preserved," from Old French memorial "record, report," and directly from Late Latin memoriale "a memorial," noun use of neuter of Latin memorialis (adj.) "of or belonging to memory," from memoria "memory" (see memory).

meanings.definitionCn: 捏；勒索；使苦恼；掐掉某物，修剪

etymology.story: late 15c., "critical juncture" (as in baseball pinch hitter, attested from 1912), from pinch (v.).

morphology.mnemonic: project, throw + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: early 15c., "action of handing over to another," from Anglo-French delivrée, noun use of fem.

meanings.definitionCn: 选举； 选择权； 当选
博学的；有学问的；学术上的

etymology.story: "having knowledge gained by study," mid-14c., past participle adjective from learn (v.) in its former transitive sense.

meanings.definitionCn: 债券；结合；约定；粘合剂；纽带
把……作为目标；规定……的指标；瞄准某物

etymology.story: "grow bigger or greater," Old English weaxan "to increase, grow" (class VII strong verb; past tense weox, past participle weaxen), from Proto-Germanic *wahsan (source also of Old Saxon, Old High German wahsan, Old Norse vaxa, Old Frisian waxa, Dutch wassen, German wachsen, Gothic wahsjan "to grow, increase"), from PIE *weg- (source also of Sanskrit vaksayati "cause to grow," Greek auxein "to increase"), extended form of root *aug- (1) "to increase" (see augment).

"characteristic of city life, pertaining to cities or towns," 1610s (but rare before 1830s), from Latin urbanus "of or pertaining to a city or city life; in Rome," also "in city fashion, polished, refined, cultivated, courteous," but also sometimes "witty, facetious, bold, impudent;" as a noun, "city dweller," from urbs (genitive urbis) "city, walled town," a word of unknown origin.

root.meaning: book of information on local sites

morphology.mnemonic: book of information on local sites + ance

etymology.story: 1530s, "the process of directing conduct," hybrid from guide (v.) + -ance; replacing 15c.

meanings.definitionCn: 火焰， 火苗；热情；光辉

etymology.story: Middle English flaumen, also flaumben, flomben, flamben, flamen, flammen, c.

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 持有/保持 + er

etymology.story: mid-15c., agent noun from contain..

meanings.definitionCn: 领袖， 领导人， 首领

etymology.story: Old English lædere "one who leads, one first or most prominent," agent noun from lædan "to guide, conduct" (see lead (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 微妙的；精美的，雅致的；柔和的；易碎的；纤弱的；清淡可口的

etymology.story: late 14c., "self-indulgent, loving ease; delightful; sensitive, easily hurt; feeble," from Latin delicatus "alluring, delightful, dainty," also "addicted to pleasure, luxurious, effeminate;" which is of uncertain origin; related by folk etymology (and perhaps genuinely) to deliciae "a pet," and delicere "to allure, entice" (see delicious).

meanings.definitionCn: 抛弃；被丢弃的东西或人

etymology.story: 1590s, literally "to throw a card away," from dis- "away" + card (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 想象的；奇特的；昂贵的；精选的

etymology.story: "take a liking to," 1540s, a contraction of fantasien "to fantasize (about)," from fantasy (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 饰以薄纱；用化妆纸揩去

etymology.story: mid-14c., "band or belt of rich material," from Old French tissu "a ribbon, headband, belt of woven material" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 实验的；根据实验的；试验性的

etymology.story: mid 15c., "having experience," from experiment (n.) + -al (1).

morphology.mnemonic: to undo, untie, unfasten + 使变成/变得

etymology.story: late 14c., losnen, later lousen (early 15c.), from loose (v.) + -en (1).
mid-15c., "to rent out property, grant possession and enjoyment of in exchange for a consideration paid," from Old French renter "pay dues to," or from rent (n.1).
"close at hand," late 14c.
1560s, "having moderate self-regard," from Middle French modeste (14c.), from Latin modestus "keeping due measure" (see modesty).

meanings.definitionCn: 投，抛；计算；浇铸；投射（光、影、视线等）
投掷，抛；铸件，[古生] 铸型；演员阵容；脱落物
焦虑的；担忧的；渴望的；急切的

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin anxius "solicitous, uneasy, troubled in mind" (also "causing anxiety, troublesome"), from angere, anguere "choke, squeeze," figuratively "torment, cause distress" (see anger (v.)).

early 13c., from hate (v.) + rare suffix -red (indicating condition or state), from Old English ræden "state, condition," related to verb rædan "to advise, discuss, rule, read, guess." See read (v.) and compare the second element of kindred and proper names Æþelræd and Alfred..

meanings.definitionCn: 粉碎；迷恋；压榨；拥挤的人群

root.meaning: free or at liberty in a general way (without particulars)

morphology.mnemonic: free or at liberty in a general way (without particulars) + 以…方式/副词化

meanings.definitionCn: 切下；把…分成部分；将…切成薄片

etymology.story: coined 1891 by Irish physicist George J.

morphology.mnemonic: 忠诚的，真实的 + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: Old English treowlice, from treow (see true (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 法律顾问；忠告；商议；讨论；决策

etymology.story: late 13c., from Old French conseiller "to advise, counsel," from Latin consiliari, from consilium "plan, opinion" (see counsel (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 条款，项目；一则；一件商品（或物品）

etymology.story: late 14c., originally an adverb, "moreover, in addition," from Latin item (adv.) "likewise, just so, moreover," probably from ita "thus," id "it" (see id) + adverbial ending -tem (compare idem "the same").
1911, from German Gen, coined 1905 by Danish scientist Wilhelm Ludvig Johannsen (1857-1927), from Greek genea "generation, race," from PIE root *gene- (see genus).

meanings.definitionCn: 自杀；给自己带来恶劣后果的行为

etymology.story: "deliberate killing of oneself," 1650s, from Modern Latin suicidium "suicide," from Latin sui "of oneself" (genitive of se "self"), from PIE *s(u)w-o- "one's own," from root *s(w)e- (see idiom) + -cidium "a killing" (see -cide).
small wild plant with purplish-blue flowers, c.

meanings.definitionCn: 空的,中空的;空洞的,空虚的
树干；躯干；象鼻；汽车车尾的行李箱
节约的；挽救的；补偿的；保留的

etymology.story: late 14c., from safe (adj.); see save (prep.)..

early 14c., "to gather, assemble" (transitive and intransitive), from Old French relier "assemble, put together; fasten, attach, rally, oblige," from Latin religare "fasten, bind fast," from re-, intensive prefix (see re-), + ligare "to bind" (see ligament).

root.meaning: institution for sick or wounded people

morphology.mnemonic: institution for sick or wounded people + ize

etymology.story: 1873, from hospital + -ize.

meanings.definitionCn: 滑动；滑落；不知不觉陷入

etymology.story: 1794, from French utiliser, from Italian utilizzare, from utile "usable," from Latin utilis "usable," from uti "make use of, profit by, take advantage of" (see use (v.))..
1727, "adhesive, inclined to stick," from stick (v.) + -y (2).

morphology.mnemonic: 在…之前/预先 + 强壮/价值

etymology.story: late 14c., "middle part of the body," also "part of a garment fitted for the waist, portion of a garment that covers the waist" (but, due to fashion styles, often above or below it), probably from Old English *wæst "growth," hence, "where the body grows," from Proto-Germanic *wahs-tu- (source also of Old English wæstm, Old Norse vöxtr, Swedish växt, Old High German wahst "growth, increase," Gothic wahstus "stature," Old English weaxan "to grow" see wax (v.)), from PIE *wegs-, extended form of root *aug- (1) "to increase" (see augment)..

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 走/让步 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French excessif "excessive, oppressive," from Latin excess-, past participle stem of excedere "to depart, go beyond" (see exceed).
1520s, "to render (a hunting animal) eager for prey by rewarding it with flesh from a kill," with figurative extensions, from flesh (n.).

1776, "dense growth of trees and other tangled vegetation," such as that of the swampy regions at the base of the Himalayas in India, from Hindi jangal "desert, forest, wasteland, uncultivated ground," from Sanskrit jangala-s "arid, sparsely grown with trees," a word of unknown origin.
"to divide in portions," early 14c., from Old French porcioner "share out, divide in portions," from porcion (see portion (n.)).

"long bench," 1550s, from Middle English setle "a seat," from Old English setl "a seat, stall; position, abode; setting of a heavenly body," related to sittan "to sit," from Proto-Germanic *setla- (source also of Middle Low German, Middle Dutch setel, Dutch zetel, German Sessel, Gothic sitls), from PIE *sedla- (source also of Latin sella "seat, chair," Old Church Slavonic sedlo "saddle," Old English sadol "saddle"), from root *sed- (1) "to sit" (see sedentary)..

meanings.definitionCn: 严厉的；严酷的；刺耳的；粗糙的；刺目的
[车辆] 车辆；工具；交通工具；运载工具；传播媒介；媒介物

etymology.story: 1610s, "a medium through which a drug or medicine is administered," also "any means of conveying or transmitting," from French véhicule (16c.), from Latin vehiculum "means of transport, vehicle, carriage, conveyance," from vehere "to bear, carry, convey," from PIE *wegh- "to go, transport in a vehicle" (source also of Old English wegan "to carry;" Old Norse vegr, Old High German weg "way;" Middle Dutch wagen "wagon;" see wagon).

root.meaning: with effort or energy, with difficulty

morphology.mnemonic: with effort or energy, with difficulty + 身份/状态/技能

etymology.story: 1550s, in literal sense "mix or mingle things so as to render the elements indistinguishable;" attested from mid-18c.

meanings.definitionCn: 垫子；起缓解作用之物；（猪等的）臀肉；银行储蓄
给…安上垫子；把…安置在垫子上；缓和…的冲击

etymology.story: late 13c., from Anglo-French glorious, Old French glorieus "glorious, blessed" (12c., Modern French glorieux), from Latin gloriosus "full of glory, famous," from gloria (see glory (n.)).

early 13c., fisicien "a healer, a medical practitioner," from Old French fisiciien "physician, doctor, sage" (12c., Modern French physicien means "physicist"), from fisique "art of healing," from Latin physica "natural science" (see physic).

meanings.definitionCn: 建筑，建筑学；建筑式样或风格，建筑物

morphology.mnemonic: 统治/首领 + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: 1560s, from Middle French architecture, from Latin architectura, from architectus "architect" (see architect)..

meanings.definitionCn: 压力；强调；紧张；重要性；重读
强调；使紧张；加压力于；用重音读
紧握；柄；支配；握拍方式；拍柄绷带

etymology.story: 1580s, "to investigate, examine," a back-formation from exploration, or else from Middle French explorer (16c.), from Latin explorare "investigate, search out, examine, explore," said to be originally a hunters' term meaning "set up a loud cry," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + plorare "to weep, cry."

meanings.definitionCn: 电力；电流；强烈的紧张情绪

etymology.story: 1640s (Browne, from Gilbert's Modern Latin), from electric (q.v.) + -ity.

late 14c., "horizontal zone of the earth," Scottish, from Old French climat "region, part of the earth," from Latin clima (genitive climatis) "region; slope of the Earth," from Greek klima "region, zone," literally "an inclination, slope," thus "slope of the Earth from equator to pole," from root of klinein "to slope, to lean" (see lean (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 管理者；售票员；领队，指挥；导体

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 引导 + …者/…器

etymology.story: 1520s, "one who leads or guides," from Middle French conductour (14c., Old French conduitor), from Latin conductor "one who hires, contractor," in Late Latin "a carrier," from conductus, past participle of conducere (see conduce).

meanings.definitionCn: 地壳；外壳；面包皮；坚硬外皮

etymology.story: late 14c.; see crust (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 混乱,杂乱;骚乱;失调,疾病

root.meaning: to give orders for or to

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + to give orders for or to

root.meaning: one who judges merits of books, plays, etc.

morphology.mnemonic: one who judges merits of books, plays, etc. + ize

etymology.story: 1640s, "to pass judgment on something" (usually unfavorable), from critic + -ize.

morphology.mnemonic: musical instrument, + ize + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., "act of organizing," from Middle French organisation and directly from Medieval Latin organizationem (nominative organizatio), noun of action from past participle stem of organizare, from Latin organum "instrument, organ" (see organ).

meanings.definitionCn: 愤怒；狂暴，肆虐；情绪激动

etymology.story: 1620s, formed from wide on model of breadth, and replacing wideness (Old English widnes).

meanings.definitionCn: 奶制品；乳牛；制酪场；乳品店；牛奶及乳品业
乳品的；牛奶的；牛奶制的；产乳的

etymology.story: late 13c., "building for making butter and cheese; dairy farm," formed with Anglo-French -erie affixed to Middle English daie (in daie maid "dairymaid"), from Old English dæge "kneader of bread, housekeeper, female servant" (see dey (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 上（飞机、车、船等）；用板盖上；给提供膳宿

etymology.story: verb senses derived from various senses of board (n.1) and board (n.2) include "come alongside" (a ship), mid-15c.

1590s, "pertaining to management of a household," perhaps shortened from economical, or else from French économique or directly from Latin oeconomicus "of domestic economy," from Greek oikonomikos "practiced in the management of a household or family" (also the name of a treatise by Xenophon on the duties of domestic life), hence, "frugal, thrifty," from oikonomia "household management" (see economy (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 东西；材料；填充物；素材资料

etymology.story: early 14c., "quilted material worn under chain mail," from Old French estoffe "quilted material, furniture, provisions" (Modern French étoffe), from estoffer "to equip or stock," which according to French sources is from Old High German stopfon "to plug, stuff," or from a related Frankish word (see stop (v.)), but OED has "strong objections" to this.
late 14c., from Anglo-French arest, Old French areste (n.), from arester "to stay, stop" (see arrest (v.))..
early 14c.; see widow (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 区别；差别；特性；荣誉、勋章
成熟的；充分考虑的；到期的；成年人的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "ripe," also "careful, well-considered," from Latin maturus "ripe, timely, early" (see mature (v.))..

morphology.mnemonic: 船 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: early 14c., "fleet of ships," especially for purposes of war, from Old French navie "fleet; ship," from Latin navigia, plural of navigium "vessel, boat," from navis "ship" (see naval).

Middle English, representing the merger or mutual influence of two words, one from Old English, one from an Old French form of the same Germanic word, and both ultimately from a PIE root meaning "cattle."

The Old English word is feoh "livestock, cattle; movable property; possessions in livestock, goods, or money; riches, treasure, wealth; money as a medium of exchange or payment," from Proto-Germanic *fehu- (source also of Old Saxon fehu, Old High German fihu, German Vieh "cattle," Gothic faihu "money, fortune").

meanings.definitionCn: 章节；部分；地区；部门， 科 A

morphology.mnemonic: cut切割 + 状态/行为/过程

meanings.definitionCn: 影响；势力；感化；有影响的人或事
抽打；煽动；搅打（蛋，奶油）；彻底击败
鞭子；抽打；车夫；[机] 搅拌器

etymology.story: 1540s, as prothogall "draft of a document," from Middle French prothocole (c.

Old English hwæl "whale," also "walrus," from Proto-Germanic *hwalaz (source also of Old Saxon hwal, Old Norse hvalr, hvalfiskr, Swedish val, Middle Dutch wal, walvisc, Dutch walvis, Old High German wal, German Wal), from PIE *(s)kwal-o- (source also of Latin squalus "a kind of large sea fish").

late 14c., from Old French provoker, provochier (12c., Modern French provoquer) and directly from Latin provocare "call forth, challenge," from pro- "forth" (see pro-) + vocare "to call" (see voice (n.)).
late 13c., "attempt to influence by menacing," from Old English þreatnian "to threaten" (see threat).

late 13c., "inclination, disposition, will, desire," from Old French talent (12c.), from Medieval Latin talenta, plural of talentum "inclination, leaning, will, desire" (11c.), in classical Latin "balance, weight; sum of money," from Greek talanton "a balance, pair of scales," hence "weight, definite weight, anything weighed," and in later times sum of money," from PIE *tele- "to lift, support, weigh," "with derivatives referring to measured weights and thence money and payment" [Watkins]; see extol.
1789, from percent + -age.
"to communicate in search of mutual agreement," 1590s, back-formation from negotiation, or else from Latin negotiatus, past participle of negotiari.

meanings.definitionCn: 张力；拉紧；负担；扭伤；血缘
打猎； 猎取；搜索； 寻找
打猎， 猎取；搜索； 寻找

etymology.story: 1570s, from Italian violino, diminutive of viola (see viola).

meanings.definitionCn: 责备，告诫；抗议；表示异议

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + animal of vast size + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1590s, "make plain," back-formation from remonstration, or else from Medieval Latin remonstratus, past participle of  remonstrare "to demonstrate" (see remonstrance).

meanings.definitionCn: 包，包裹；套装软件，[计] 程序包

morphology.mnemonic: 和平 + 行为/结果/集合

meanings.definitionCn: 看得见的, 明显的, 显著的

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French visable, visible "perceptible" (12c.) and directly from Latin visibilis "that may be seen," from visus, past participle of videre "to see" (see vision).

early 15c., from Latinized form of Greek krisis "turning point in a disease" (used as such by Hippocrates and Galen), literally "judgment, result of a trial, selection," from krinein "to separate, decide, judge," from PIE root *krei- "to sieve, discriminate, distinguish" (source also of Greek krinesthai "to explain;" Old English hriddel "sieve;" Latin cribrum "sieve," crimen "judgment, crime," cernere (past participle cretus) "to sift, separate;" Old Irish criathar, Old Welsh cruitr "sieve;" Middle Irish crich "border, boundary").

meanings.definitionCn: 幸存,生存;幸存者,残存物

morphology.mnemonic: 超过/在…上 + long live ______; + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1590s, "act of surviving; continuation after some event," from survive + -al (2).

mid-15c., "bud, sprout;" 1640s, "rudiment of a new organism in an existing one," from Middle French germe "germ (of egg); bud, seed, fruit; offering," from Latin germen (genitive germinis) "spring, offshoot; sprout, bud," which is of uncertain origin, perhaps from PIE root *gen-, *gene- "to beget, bear" (see german (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 内部的， 里面的；内心的
敏锐的，敏捷的；渴望的；强烈的；热心的；锐利的

etymology.story: "lament loudly over the dead, bitterly wail," 1811, from Irish caoinim "I weep, wail, lament," from Old Irish coinim "I wail." Hence "to utter in a shrill voice" (1893).
early 13c., "footprint," from foot (n.) + step (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 老兵；老手；富有经验的人；老运动员
漠不关心的；无关紧要的；中性的，中立的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + day日 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: late 14c., "unbiased, impartial, not preferring one to the other" (of persons), "alike, equal" (of things), from Old French indifferent "impartial" or directly from Latin indifferentem (nominative indifferens) "not differing, not particular, of no consequence, neither good nor evil," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + differens, present participle of differre "set apart" (see differ).

meanings.definitionCn: 爱，喜爱；爱慕之情；感情；疾病，不适

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 做/制造 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 13c., "an emotion of the mind, passion, lust as opposed to reason," from Old French afection (12c.) "emotion, inclination, disposition; love, attraction, enthusiasm," from Latin affectionem (nominative affectio) "a relation, disposition; a temporary state; a frame, constitution," noun of state from past participle stem of afficere "to do something to, act on" (see affect (n.)).
mid-14c., from Old French egle, from Old Provençal aigla, from Latin aquila "black eagle," fem.
1650s, from Dutch kruisen "to cross, sail to and fro," from kruis "cross," from Latin crux.

meanings.definitionCn: 基本的；初级的；[化学] 元素的

etymology.story: late 14c., "having the nature of one of the four elements," from Middle French elementaire and directly from Latin elementarius "belonging to the elements or rudiments," from elementum (see element).

meanings.definitionCn: 主题（等于theme）；题目；一般规则；总论

etymology.story: 1630s, "a class of considerations from which probable arguments can be drawn," singular form of "Topics" (1560s), the name of a work by Aristotle on logical and rhetorical generalities, from Latin Topica, from Greek Ta Topika, literally "matters concerning topoi," "commonplaces," neuter plural of noun use of topikos "pertaining to a common place, of a place, local," from topos "place" (see topos).

mid-14c., "performed with due religious ceremony or reverence, sacred, devoted to religious observances," also, of a vow, etc., "made under religious sanction, binding," from Old French solempne (12c., Modern French solennel) and directly from Latin sollemnis "annual, established, religiously fixed, formal, ceremonial, traditional," perhaps related to sollus "whole" (see safe (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 省略， 删去；遗漏， 忽略 <

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin omittere "let go, let fall," figuratively "lay aside, disregard," from assimilated form of ob (here perhaps intensive) + mittere "let go, send" (see mission).

meanings.definitionCn: 使困窘,使局促不安;阻碍,麻烦

etymology.story: 1670s, "perplex, throw into doubt," from French embarrasser (16c.), literally "to block," from Italian imbarrazzo, from imbarrare "to bar," from assimilated form of in- "into, upon" (see in- (2)) + Vulgar Latin *barra "bar" (see bar (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: [计] 选项；选择权；买卖的特权

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French erroneus and directly from Latin erroneus "vagrant, wandering" (in Late Latin "erroneous"), from erronem (nominative erro) "a wanderer, vagabond," from past participle stem of errare "to wander; to err" (see err).
1520s, "science of government," from politic (adj.), modeled on Aristotle's ta politika "affairs of state," the name of his book on governing and governments, which was in English mid-15c.

meanings.definitionCn: 严重的，[医] 急性的；敏锐的；激烈的；尖声的

etymology.story: late 14c., originally of fevers and diseases, "coming and going quickly" (opposed to a chronic), from Latin acutus "sharp, pointed," figuratively "shrill, penetrating; intelligent, cunning," past participle of acuere "sharpen" (see acuity).
late 14c., from Old French limitacion and directly from Latin limitationem (nominative limitatio), noun of action from past participle stem of limitare (see limit (v.)).

1852, from German Kinder-Garten (1840), literally "children-garden, garden of children," a metaphoric name from Kinder "children" (plural of Kind "child;" see kin (n.)) + Garten "garden" (see yard (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 想不到的, 意外的, 未预料到

root.meaning: count upon (to do something), trust or rely on

morphology.mnemonic: 不/非/否定 + count upon (to do something), trust or rely on

etymology.story: 1580s, from un- (1) "not" + past participle of expect (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 重要的；决定性的；定局的；决断的

etymology.story: 1706, "cross-shaped," from French crucial, a medical term for ligaments of the knee (which cross each other), from Latin crux (genitive crucis) "cross" (see cross (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 给予/分配

etymology.story: 1520s, from Latin contributus, past participle of contribuere "to bring together, add, unite, collect, contribute" (see contribution).

mid-13c., faith, feith, fei, fai "faithfulness to a trust or promise; loyalty to a person; honesty, truthfulness," from Anglo-French and Old French feid, foi "faith, belief, trust, confidence; pledge" (11c.), from Latin fides "trust, faith, confidence, reliance, credence, belief," from root of fidere "to trust," from PIE root *bheidh- "to trust" (source also of Greek pistis "faith, confidence, honesty;" see bid).
1640s, "person who begets, causes, or produces," from Latin generator "a begetter, producer," agent noun from past participle stem of generare "to bring forth" (see generation).

meanings.definitionCn: 插入物；管芯；镶块；[机械]刀片
禁止；不准；不允许；〈正式〉严禁

etymology.story: Old English forbeodan "forbid, prohibit" (past tense forbead, plural forbudon, past participle forboden), from for- "against" + beodan "to command" (see bid (v.)).
1650s, from Modern Latin microscopium, literally "an instrument for viewing what is small," from Greek micro- (see micro-) + -skopion, from skopein "to look, see" (see -scope)..

morphology.mnemonic: 必要/不可避免 + …的场所/与…有关 + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: mid-15c., "inevitably, unavoidably," from necessary (adj.) + -ly (2)..

morphology.mnemonic: 发光/闪耀 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1680s, from French billion (originally byllion in Chuquet's unpublished "Le Triparty en la Science des Nombres," 1484; copied by De la Roche, 1520); see bi- "two" + million.

meanings.definitionCn: 平行的,相同的,类似的n.平行线,类似,对比
拒绝,抵制,丢弃,排斥,退掉

etymology.story: "suffering or sick person under medical treatment," late 14c., from Old French pacient (n.), from the adjective, from Latin patientem (see patience)..

"dismiss," 1590s, from Middle Dutch casseren, kaseeren "to cast off, discharge," from French casser "to discharge, annul," from Late Latin cassare "annul," from Latin cassus "void, empty" (see caste (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 绝对的；透明的；峻峭的；纯粹的

etymology.story: 1620s, "deviate from course" (of a ship), of obscure origin, perhaps from Dutch scheren "to move aside, withdraw, depart," originally "to separate" (see shear (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 奇迹,令人惊奇的人(或事)

etymology.story: mid-12c., "a wondrous work of God," from Old French miracle (11c.) "miracle, story of a miracle, miracle play," from Latin miraculum "object of wonder" (in Church Latin, "marvelous event caused by God"), from mirari "to wonder at, marvel, be astonished," figuratively "to regard, esteem," from mirus "wonderful, astonishing, amazing," earlier *smeiros, from PIE *smei- "to smile, laugh" (source also of Sanskrit smerah "smiling," Greek meidan "to smile," Old Church Slavonic smejo "to laugh;" see smile (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 原始的；最初的；独创的；新颖的

morphology.mnemonic: 起源/开始 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: "original text," late 14c., from Medieval Latin originale (see original (adj.)).

"to know" (archaic), Old English witan (past tense wast, past participle witen) "to know, beware of or conscious of, understand, observe, ascertain, learn," from Proto-Germanic *witan "to have seen," hence "to know" (source also of Old Saxon witan, Old Norse vita, Old Frisian wita, Middle Dutch, Dutch weten, Old High German wizzan, German wissen, Gothic witan "to know"), from PIE *weid- (see wit (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: to pass (something to someone) + 充满…的

etymology.story: Old English handful "as much as can be held in the open hand;" see hand (n.) + -ful.

meanings.definitionCn: 分配，指派；(指定的)作业，任务

morphology.mnemonic: to fix, settle, determine, appoint + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: late 14c., "order, request, directive," from Old French assignement "(legal) assignment (of dower, etc.)," from Late Latin assignamentum, noun of action from Latin assignare (see assign).

meanings.definitionCn: 尼龙，[纺] 聚酰胺纤维；尼龙袜

etymology.story: 1938, coined, according to DuPont, from random generic syllable nyl- + -on, common ending in fiber names (compare rayon), said to be ultimately from cotton.

meanings.definitionCn: 电的；电动的；发电的；导电的；令人震惊的

etymology.story: 1640s, first used in English by physician Sir Thomas Browne (1605-1682), apparently coined as Modern Latin electricus (literally "resembling amber") by English physicist William Gilbert (1540-1603) in treatise "De Magnete" (1600), from Latin electrum "amber," from Greek elektron "amber" (Homer, Hesiod, Herodotus), also "pale gold" (a compound of 1 part silver to 4 of gold); which is of unknown origin.

morphology.mnemonic: 终止/界限 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: Old English cyningdom; see king (n.) + -dom.

meanings.definitionCn: 各种各样的； 不同的； 多方面的

etymology.story: early 15c., "characterized by variety," from Middle French varieux and directly from Latin varius "changing, different, diverse" (see vary).
Old English lætra "slower," comparative of læt "late" (see late (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 忧愁， 消沉； 低气压， 低压； 不景气， 萧条

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + device for pressing cloth + 状态/行为/过程
再/重新/回 + 引导 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "a restoring to a former state; a subjugation" (of a people, etc.), from Middle French reducion (13c., Modern French réduction) and directly from Latin reductionem (nominative reductio) "a leading back, restoration," noun of action from past participle stem of reducere (see reduce).

meanings.definitionCn: 广度， 宽度， 长度；程度， 限度

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 伸展/趋向

etymology.story: early 14c., from Anglo-French extente, estente "extent, extension;" in law, "valuation of land, stretch of land," from fem.

early 15c., from Middle French equivalent and directly from Late Latin aequivalentem (nominative aequivalens) "equivalent," present participle of aequivalere "be equivalent," from Latin aequus "equal" (see equal (adj.)) + valere "be well, be worth" (see valiant).

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + metrical composition + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: Old English bannan "to summon, command, proclaim," from Proto-Germanic *bannan "proclaim, command, forbid" (source also of Old High German bannan "to command or forbid under threat of punishment," German bannen "banish, expel, curse"), originally "to speak publicly," from PIE root *bha- (2) "to speak" (source also of Old Irish bann "law," Armenian ban "word;" see fame (n.)).

1590s, "bewitch, enchant," from Middle French fasciner (14c.), from Latin fascinatus, past participle of fascinare "bewitch, enchant, fascinate," from fascinus "a charm, enchantment, spell, witchcraft," which is of uncertain origin.

mid-13c., "allow to occur or continue, permit, tolerate, fail to prevent or suppress," also "to be made to undergo, endure, be subjected to" (pain, death, punishment, judgment, grief), from Anglo-French suffrir, Old French sofrir "bear, endure, resist; permit, tolerate, allow" (Modern French souffrir), from Vulgar Latin *sufferire, variant of Latin sufferre "to bear, undergo, endure, carry or put under," from sub "up, under" (see sub-) + ferre "to carry" (see infer).

meanings.definitionCn: 外交的；外交上的；老练的

etymology.story: 1711, "pertaining to documents, texts, charters," from Medieval Latin diplomaticus, from diplomat-, stem of diploma (see diploma).

meanings.definitionCn: 邻居； 四邻； 街道， 住宅区
汇票；草稿；选派；（尤指房间、烟囱、炉子等供暖系统中的）（小股）气流
初步画出或（写出）的；（设计、草图、提纲或版本）正在起草中的，草拟的；以草稿形式的；草图的
复杂的；合成的， 综合的

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 折叠/交织

meanings.definitionCn: 假定，设想；采取；承担；推测；假装
巨人；伟人；[动] 巨大的动物
流动的；流畅的；不固定的
出口，排放孔；[电] 电源插座；销路；发泄的方法；批发商店

etymology.story: mid-13c., "a river mouth," from out + let (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 加，加法；附加部分，增加(物)

morphology.mnemonic: to put, place, + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "action of adding numbers;" c.

meanings.definitionCn: 使成群结队；结伙伤害或恐吓某人
起皱； 收缩；退缩， 畏缩

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin textilis "a web, canvas, woven fabric, cloth, something woven," noun use of textilis "woven, wrought," from texere "to weave," from PIE root *teks- "to make" (see texture (n.)).
"one who gives form," mid-14c., agent noun from form (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 延伸；扩大；推广；伸出；给予；使竭尽全力；对…估价

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 伸展/趋向

etymology.story: early 14c., "to value, assess," from Anglo-French estendre (late 13c.), Old French estendre "stretch out, extend, increase," transitive and intransitive (Modern French étendre), from Latin extendere "stretch out, spread out; increase, enlarge, prolong, continue," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + tendere "to stretch" (see tenet).

Old English faldan (Mercian), fealdan (West Saxon), transitive, "to bend (cloth) back over itself, wrap up, furl," class VII strong verb (past tense feold, past participle fealden), from Proto-Germanic *falthan, *faldan (source also of Middle Dutch vouden, Dutch vouwen, Old Norse falda, Middle Low German volden, Old High German faldan, German falten, Gothic falþan).

meanings.definitionCn: 塑料的， 塑胶的；可塑的， 塑性的

etymology.story: late 14c., "a large mass;" mid-15c., "spherical solid body, a sphere," from Middle French globe (14c.) and directly from Latin globus "round mass, sphere, ball" (also, of men, "a throng, crowd, body, mass"), which is related to gleba "clod, lump of soil" (see glebe) and perhaps glomus "a ball, ball of yarn," but de Vaan says the last two probably are non-IE loan-words.

morphology.mnemonic: chem + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 口译； 解释， 诠释； 艺术处理

etymology.story: Old English folc "common people, laity; men; people, nation, tribe; multitude; troop, army," from Proto-Germanic *folkam (source also of Old Saxon folc, Old Frisian folk, Middle Dutch volc, Dutch volk, Old High German folc, German Volk "people").

meanings.definitionCn: 需要考虑的事;考虑,思考;体谅,照顾

morphology.mnemonic: stretch, extend, + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-14c., "a beholding, looking at," also "keeping in mind," from Old French consideracion (12c., Modern French considération), from Latin considerationem (nominative consideratio) "consideration, contemplation, reflection," noun of action from past participle stem of considerare (see consider).

meanings.definitionCn: 竞争的；好竞争的；有竞争力的

etymology.story: 1826, from Latin competit-, past participle stem of competere (see compete) + -ive.

meanings.definitionCn: 打听， 询问；调查， 研究

etymology.story: alternative spelling of inquiry.
early 15c., from Latin applausus, past participle of applaudere "approve by clapping hands" (see applaud)..

"to free a letter for carriage or an article for publication, to send by public conveyance free of expense," 1708, from shortened form of French affranchir, from a- "to" + franchir "to free" (see franchise (v.)).
mid-15c., "improper practice," from Old French abus (14c.), from Latin abusus (see abuse (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 证明,证实;检验;考验;鉴定;结果是,表明是

etymology.story: late 12c., pruven, proven "to try, test; evaluate; demonstrate," from Old French prover, pruver "show; convince; put to the test" (11c., Modern French prouver), from Latin probare "to make good; esteem, represent as good; make credible, show, demonstrate; test, inspect; judge by trial" (source also of Spanish probar, Italian probare), from probus "worthy, good, upright, virtuous," from PIE *pro-bhwo- "being in front," from *pro-, extended form of root *per- (1) "forward, through" (see per), + root *bhu- "to be" (source also of Latin fui "I have been," futurus "about to be;" Old English beon "to be;" see be).

late 14c., "mental state," from Latin noun use of affectus "furnished, supplied, endowed," figuratively "disposed, constituted, inclined," past participle of afficere "to do; treat, use, manage, handle; act on; have influence on, do something to," a verb of broad meaning, from ad- "to" (see ad-) + facere (past participle factus) "to make, do" (see factitious).

Old English deaf "deaf," also "empty, barren," specialized from Proto-Germanic *daubaz (source also of Old Saxon dof, Old Norse daufr, Old Frisian daf, Dutch doof "deaf," German taub, Gothic daufs "deaf, insensate"), from PIE dheubh-, which was used to form words meaning "confusion, stupefaction, dizziness" (source also of Greek typhlos "blind," typhein "to make smoke;" Old English dumb "unable to speak;" Old High German tumb).

meanings.definitionCn: 钦佩,赞赏,羡慕；称赞,夸奖

morphology.mnemonic: an event which follows another + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: late 14c., "venomous substance," from Latin virus "poison, sap of plants, slimy liquid, a potent juice," probably from PIE root *weis- "to melt away, to flow," used of foul or malodorous fluids, with specialization in some languages to "poisonous fluid" (source also of Sanskrit visam "poison," visah "poisonous;" Avestan vish- "poison;" Latin viscum "sticky substance, birdlime;" Greek ios "poison," ixos "mistletoe, birdlime;" Old Church Slavonic višnja "cherry;" Old Irish fi "poison;" Welsh gwy "fluid, water," gwyar "blood").

meanings.definitionCn: 排水；下水道，排水管；消耗

etymology.story: "to set or place apart, to detach so as to make alone," by 1786, a back-formation from isolated (q.v.).

root.meaning: to set out on a sea voyage, leave port

morphology.mnemonic: to set out on a sea voyage, leave port + …者/…器

meanings.definitionCn: 娱乐；消遣；使发笑；使愉快

etymology.story: late 15c., "to divert the attention, beguile, delude," from Middle French amuser "divert, cause to muse," from a "at, to" (but here probably a causal prefix) + muser "ponder, stare fixedly" (see muse (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 在内的， 里面的； 内心的 <

morphology.mnemonic: 完整 + to parry, to fend off

etymology.story: Old English inweard "inmost; sincere; internal, intrinsic; deep," from Proto-Germanic *inwarth "inward" (source also of Old Norse innanverðr, Old High German inwart, Middle Dutch inwaert), from root of Old English inne "in" (see in (adv.)) + -weard (see -ward).
"condition of being responsible," 1787, from responsible + -ity.
alternative form of inquire, according to OED mainly used in sense of "to ask a question."

meanings.definitionCn: 公民的；民间的；文职的；有礼貌的；根据民法的

etymology.story: late 14c., "relating to civil law or life; pertaining to the internal affairs of a state," from Old French civil "civil, relating to civil law" (13c.) and directly from Latin civilis "relating to a citizen, relating to public life, befitting a citizen," hence by extension "popular, affable, courteous;" alternative adjectival derivation of civis "townsman" (see city).

morphology.mnemonic: 人/民众 + 与…有关的/像…的 + ity

etymology.story: "fact or condition of being beloved by the people," c.

"following in time, later," mid-15c., from Middle French subsequent (14c.) and directly from Latin subsequentem (nominative subsequens), present participle of subsequi "come after in time, follow closely," figuratively "imitate, conform to," from sub "closely, up to" (see sub-) + sequi "follow" (see suit (n.)).

1650s, "to point out," back-formation from indication (q.v.) or else from Latin indicatus, past participle of indicare "to point out, show, indicate." Especially "to give suggestion of, be reason for inferring" (1706).

meanings.definitionCn: 几乎不，简直不；简直没有
面,板;控制板,仪表盘;专门小组
突出的， 杰出的； 未解决的； 未偿付的

morphology.mnemonic: 超过/外部 + place of standing, position

etymology.story: 1610s, "projecting, prominent, detached," present participle adjective from outstand (v.) "endure successfully, hold out against," from out (adv.) + stand (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 费用；电荷；掌管；控告；命令；负载
使充电；使承担；指责；装载；对…索费；向…冲去

etymology.story: Old English siwian "to stitch, sew, mend, patch, knit together," earlier siowian, from Proto-Germanic *siwjanan (source also of Old Norse syja, Swedish sy, Danish sye, Old Frisian sia, Old High German siuwan, Gothic siujan "to sew"), from PIE root *syu- "to bind, sew" (source also of Sanskrit sivyati "sews," sutram "thread, string;" Greek hymen "thin skin, membrane," hymnos "song;" Latin suere "to sew, sew together;" Old Church Slavonic šijo "to sew," šivu "seam;" Lettish siuviu, siuti "to sew," siuvikis "tailor;" Russian švec "tailor").
1560s, from Middle French ovalle "oval figure," from Medieval Latin ovalis (see oval (adj.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 纵队，列；专栏；圆柱，柱形物

etymology.story: mid-15c., "vertical division of a page," also "a pillar, post," from Old French colombe (12c., Modern French colonne "column, pillar"), from Latin columna "pillar," collateral form of columen "top, summit," from PIE root *kel- (4) "to project, be prominent" (see hill).
1610s, "fact or manner of proceeding," from French procédure "manner of proceeding" (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 样品,实例,标本,抽样检查
使…完整；使…成整体；求…的积分；表示…的总和

etymology.story: 1630s, "to render (something) whole, bring together the parts of," from Latin integratus, past participle of integrare "make whole," from integer "whole, complete," figuratively, "untainted, upright," literally "untouched," from in- "not" (see in- (1)) + root of tangere "to touch," from PIE root *tag- "to touch, handle" (see tangent (adj.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 超过/在…上 + long live ______; + …者/…器

etymology.story: "one who applies," late 15c., from Latin applicantem (nominative applicans), present participle of applicare (see apply)..

root.meaning: region between the tropics of Cancer and Capricorn

morphology.mnemonic: region between the tropics of Cancer and Capricorn + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1520s, "pertaining to the celestial tropics," from tropic + -al (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 合作者,合伙人,合股人,伙伴,舞伴,配偶
突然地下降；投入；陷入；跳进

etymology.story: late 14c., spydyr, from earlier spiþre, spiþur, spiþer (14c.), from Old English spiðra, from Proto-Germanic *spin-thron- (cognate with Danish spinder), literally "the spinner," from *spen-wo- "to spin" (see spin (v.)) + formative or agential *-thro.

meanings.definitionCn: 采访；接见；对…进行面谈；对某人进行面试

root.meaning: ingredient which gives something its particular character

morphology.mnemonic: ingredient which gives something its particular character + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: mid-14c., "that is such by its essence," from Late Latin essentialis, from essentia "essence" (see essence).

early 14c., "make illustrious, make known," from Old French clarifiier "clarify, make clear, explain" (12c.), from Late Latin clarificare "to make clear," also "to glorify," from Latin clarificus "brilliant," from clarus "clear, distinct" (see clear (adj.)) + root of facere "to make, do" (see factitious).

early 13c., from Old French fornais "oven, furnace," figuratively "flame of love" (12c.), from Latin fornacem (nominative fornax) "an oven, kiln," related to fornus/furnus "oven," and to formus "warm," from PIE root *gwher- "warm" (source also of Greek thermos, Old English wearm; see warm (adj.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 在…上掘沟；把…开入沟里；丢弃

etymology.story: "covering over part of a ship," mid-15c., perhaps a shortening of Middle Low German verdeck (or a related North Sea Germanic word), a nautical word, from ver- "fore" + decken "to cover, put under roof," from Proto-Germanic *thakjan (related to thatch, q.v.).
13c., from Old English þunrian, from the source of thunder (n.).

late 14c., "to hold to" (a manner of life or course of conduct), from Old French observer, osserver "to observe, watch over, follow" (10c.), from Latin observare "watch over, note, heed, look to, attend to, guard, regard, comply with," from ob "over" (see ob-) + servare "to watch, keep safe," from PIE root *ser- (1) "to protect." Meaning "to attend to in practice, to keep, follow" is attested from late 14c.
early 15c., "relating to the body humors," a native formation from humor (n.), or else from Middle French humoreux "damp," from Old French humor.

mid-15c., "fit out, equip, to provision" (a castle, ship, person); "provide (soldiers)," from Old French furniss-/forniss-, present participle stem of furnir/fornir "accomplish, carry out; equip, fit out; provide" (12c.), from Vulgar Latin *fornire, alteration of *fromire, from West Germanic *frumjan "forward movement, advancement" (source also of Old High German frumjan "to do, execute, provide"), from Proto-Germanic *fram- "forwards" (see from).

meanings.definitionCn: 打赌，赌注；被打赌的事物

etymology.story: 1590s, as both a verb and noun, in the argot of petty criminals, of unknown origin; probably a shortening of abet or else from obsolete beet "to make good," from Old English bætan "make better, arouse, stimulate," from Proto-Germanic *baitjan, in which case the verb would be the original.

meanings.definitionCn: 稀有的， 难得的， 珍奇的；稀薄的， 稀疏的

etymology.story: "rise up," 1833, dialectal variant of rear (v.1).
late 14c., "relief from misfortune," see discharge (v.).
mid-14c., from Latin excludere "keep out, shut out, hinder," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + claudere "to close, shut" (see close (v.)).
also scarey, "terrifying," 1580s, from scare (n.) + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 评论性的文章，评论；批评，指责，非难

root.meaning: one who judges merits of books, plays, etc.

morphology.mnemonic: one who judges merits of books, plays, etc. + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: Old English utmest (Anglian) "outermost," double superlative of ut "out" (see out (adv.)) + -most.
late 14c., "to form a mental picture (of something), imagine," from Old French imagier, from image (see image (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 带；缎带；（勋章等的）绶带；带状物；勋表
把…撕成条带；用缎带装饰

etymology.story: early 14c., ribane, from Old French riban "a ribbon," variant of ruban (13c.), of unknown origin, possibly from a Germanic compound whose second element is related to band (n.1); compare Middle Dutch ringhband "necklace." Modern spelling is from mid-16c.

"refuse, filth," 1580s; earlier "giblets, refuse of a fowl, waste parts of an animal (head, feet, etc.) used for human food" (early 15c., in early use also gabage, garbish, garbidge ), of unknown origin; OED says probably from Anglo-French "like many other words found in early cookery books." In its sense of "waste material, refuse" it has been influenced by and partly confused with garble (q.v.) in its older sense of "remove refuse material from spices;" Middle English had the derived noun garbelage but it is attested only as the action of removing the refuse, not the material itself.

late 14c., "find fault, lament," from stem of Old French complaindre "to lament" (12c.), from Vulgar Latin *complangere, originally "to beat the breast," from Latin com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + plangere "to strike, beat the breast" (see plague (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 同种类的，同性质的，有相同特征的

etymology.story: 1640s, from Medieval Latin homogeneus, from Greek homogenes "of the same kind," from homos "same" (see homo- (1)) + genos "kind, gender, race, stock" (see genus).

meanings.definitionCn: 焦虑；渴望；挂念；令人焦虑的事

etymology.story: 1520s, from Latin anxietatem (nominative anxietas) "anguish, anxiety, solicitude," noun of quality from anxius (see anxious).
late 14c., ajusten, "to correct, remedy;" reborrowed by c.

meanings.definitionCn: 宣扬， 宣传， 推广； 使普及， 使通俗化

morphology.mnemonic: 人/民众 + 与…有关的/像…的 + ize

etymology.story: "to make a complex topic intelligible to the people," 1833, from popular + -ize.

meanings.definitionCn: 朝气蓬勃的,精力旺盛的

etymology.story: late 13c., dette, from Old French dete, from Latin debitum "thing owed," neuter past participle of debere "to owe," originally, "keep something away from someone," from de- "away" (see de-) + habere "to have" (see habit (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + 冷 + er + 使…/具有…的 + …者/…器

etymology.story: 1610s, "something that cools," agent noun from refrigerate.

late 14c., "constraining power of circumstances," from Old French necessité "need, necessity; privation, poverty; distress, torment; obligation, duty" (12c.), from Latin necessitatem (nominative necessitas) "compulsion, need for attention, unavoidableness, destiny," from necesse (see necessary).

meanings.definitionCn: 使模糊；使困惑；以雾笼罩
框架；结构；[电影] 画面

morphology.mnemonic: 光 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: mid-15c., of persons; 1540s, of actions or objects, "likely to bring luck;" from luck + -y (2).

meanings.definitionCn: 冲突，不协调；碰撞声，铿锵声

late 14c., from Old French establiss-, present participle stem of establir "cause to stand still, establish, stipulate, set up, erect, build" (12c., Modern French établir), from Latin stabilire "make stable," from stabilis "stable" (see stable (adj.)).

1610s, "anything spread out;" 1640s, "act of expanding," from French expansion, from Late Latin expansionem (nominative expansio) "a spreading out," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin expandere "to spread out" (see expand)..

meanings.definitionCn: 相关的；切题的；中肯的；有重大关系的；有意义的，目的明确的

morphology.mnemonic: 提起/相关 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: "pertinent to the matter at hand," 1550s, from Middle French relevant "depending upon," originally "helpful," from Medieval Latin relevantem (nominative relevans), from stem of Latin relevare "to lessen, lighten" (see relieve).

meanings.definitionCn: 进入；入口；条目；登记；报关手续；对土地的侵占

etymology.story: 1690s, as a term in logic, "deductive," from French synthétique (17c.) and directly from Modern Latin syntheticus, from Greek synthetikos "skilled in putting together, constructive," from synthetos "put together, constructed, compounded," past participle of syntithenai "to put together" (see synthesis).
"residence of a military commander," 1640s, from head (adj.) + quarters.
"to put to proof, test the mettle of," late 15c., from Middle French essaier, from essai "trial, attempt" (see essay (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 塞住；用插头将与电源接通

etymology.story: 1630s, from French thermomètre (1620s), coined by Jesuit Father Jean Leuréchon from Greek thermos "hot" (see thermal) + metron "measure" (see meter (n.2)).

Old English bryd "bride, betrothed or newly married woman," from Proto-Germanic *bruthiz "woman being married" (source also of Old Frisian breid, Dutch bruid, Old High German brut, German Braut "bride").

etymology.story: early 15c., "pertaining to the mind," from Middle French mental, from Late Latin mentalis "of the mind," from Latin mens (genitive mentis) "mind," from PIE root *men- "to think" (source also of Sanskrit matih "thought, mind," Gothic gamunds, Old English gemynd "memory, remembrance;" see mind (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 正派的；得体的；相当好的

etymology.story: 1530s, "proper to one's station or rank," also "tasteful," from Middle French décent, or directly from Latin decentem (nominative decens) "becoming, seemly, fitting, proper," present participle of decere "to be fitting or suitable," from PIE *deke-, from root *dek- "to take, accept, to receive, greet, be suitable" (source also of Greek dokein "to appear, seem, think," dekhesthai "to accept;" Sanskrit daśasyati "shows honor, is gracious," dacati "makes offerings, bestows;" Latin docere "to teach," decus "grace, ornament").

root.meaning: to keep in store for future use

morphology.mnemonic: to keep in store for future use + 行为/结果/集合

etymology.story: 1610s, "space for storing," from store (v.) + -age.

morphology.mnemonic: run swiftly + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1862, from race (n.2) + -ial.

1590s, "slender riding whip," probably from a Flemish or Low German word akin to Middle Dutch swijch "bough, twig," or swutsche, variant of Low German zwukse "long thin stick, switch," from Germanic *swih- (source also of Old High German zwec "wooden peg," German Zweck "aim, design," originally "peg as a target," Zwick "wooden peg"), perhaps connected with PIE root *swei- (2) "to swing, bend, to turn."

The meaning "device for changing the direction of something or making or breaking a connection" is first recorded 1797.

meanings.definitionCn: 相当大(或多)的,可观的;值得考虑的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "capable of being considered," from Medieval Latin considerabilis "worthy to be considered," from Latin considerare (see consider).

meanings.definitionCn: 助动词；辅助者，辅助物；附属机构

etymology.story: Old English hælan "cure; save; make whole, sound and well," from Proto-Germanic *hailjan (source also of Old Saxon helian, Old Norse heila, Old Frisian hela, Dutch helen,  German heilen, Gothic ga-hailjan "to heal, cure"), literally "to make whole," from PIE *kailo- "whole" (see health).

meanings.definitionCn: 储备，储存；自然保护区；预备队；缄默；[金融] 储备金
不知怎么地； 设法， 以某种方式

etymology.story: 1660s, "in some way not yet known," from some + how.

砖，砖块；砖形物；心肠好的人

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/一起 + 感情/疾病 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1570s, "affinity between certain things," from Middle French sympathie (16c.) and directly from Late Latin sympathia "community of feeling, sympathy," from Greek sympatheia "fellow-feeling, community of feeling," from sympathes "having a fellow feeling, affected by like feelings," from assimilated form of syn- "together" (see syn-) + pathos "feeling" (see pathos).
early 15c., "one who squanders or wastes," agent noun from consume.

meanings.definitionCn: 高度地；非常；非常赞许地

etymology.story: Old English healice "nobly, gloriously, honorably;" see high (adj.) + -ly (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 简短介绍， 简要汇报
键入；用键盘式排字机排字

etymology.story: 1819, from key (n.1) in sense of "mechanism of a musical instrument" + board (n.1).

morphology.mnemonic: 开始/初始 + iat + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: "power of initiating," 1775, from French initiative (16c.), from Latin initiatus, past participle of initiare "to begin," from initium "a beginning" (see initial (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 收回;(from)恢复,痊愈;重新获得

etymology.story: mid-14c., "to come to an end," also "to settle, decide" (late 14c.), from Old French determiner (12c.) or directly from Latin determinare "to enclose, bound, set limits to," from de- "off" (see de-) + terminare "to mark the end or boundary," from terminus "end, limit" (see terminus).

meanings.definitionCn: 大自然； 本性； 性质

etymology.story: late 13c., "restorative powers of the body, bodily processes; powers of growth;" from Old French nature "nature, being, principle of life; character, essence," from Latin natura "course of things; natural character, constitution, quality; the universe," literally "birth," from natus "born," past participle of nasci "to be born," from PIE *gene- "to give birth, beget" (see genus).

morphology.mnemonic: medic + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: early 15c., "medical treatment of a disease or wound," from Middle French médication and directly from Latin medicationem (nominative medicatio) "healing, cure," from past participle stem of medicare, medicari "to medicate, heal, cure" (poetic and Late Latin) from medicus "physician, healing" (see medical (adj.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 训练；钻孔机；钻子；播种机

morphology.mnemonic: 理解/智力 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: late 14c., from while (q.v.) with adverbial genitive -s-, and excrescent -t- (as in amongst, amidst, betwixt)..

meanings.definitionCn: 职员，办事员；店员；书记；记账员；<古>牧师，教士
当销售员，当店员；当职员
大厅；休息室；会客室；游说议员的团体
承认；答谢；报偿；告知已收到

etymology.story: 1550s, a blend of Middle English aknow (from Old English oncnawan "understand," from on + cnawan "recognize;" see know) and Middle English knowlechen "admit, acknowledge" (c.

1520s, from Middle French équiper "to fit out," from Old French esquiper "fit out a ship, load on board" (12c.), probably from Old Norse skipa "arrange, place in order," usually "fit out a ship," but also of warriors manning a hall and trees laden with ripe fruit, from skip "ship" (see ship (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 放射物， 辐射能； 辐射

morphology.mnemonic: 射线/放射 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-15c., "act or process of radiating," from Middle French radiation and directly from Latin radiationem (nominative radiatio) "a shining, radiation," noun of action from past participle stem of radiare "to beam, shine, gleam; make beaming," from radius "beam of light; spoke of a wheel" (see radius).

morphology.mnemonic: 呼吸 + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 值得做的，值得花时间的

etymology.story: by 1660s, worth while (one-word form from late 19c.), from worth (adj.) + while (n.).

root.meaning: a fold in a length of cloth

morphology.mnemonic: in + a fold in a length of cloth + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: late 14c., from increasing (see increase (v.)) + -ly (2)..
late 14c., from Old French salade (14c.), from Vulgar Latin *salata, literally "salted," short for herba salata "salted vegetables" (vegetables seasoned with brine, a popular Roman dish), from fem.

"pertaining to strategy, characterized by strategy," 1807, from French stratégique and directly from Greek strategikos in classical use "of or for a general; fitted for command," from strategos (see strategy).

meanings.definitionCn: 容易地；乐意地；无困难地

etymology.story: "raised open platform at the entrance of a house," 1755, American and Canadian, from Dutch stoep "flight of steps, doorstep, threshold," from Middle Dutch, from Proto-Germanic *stap- "step" (see step (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 飞驰，飞快地移动；迅速增加
辉煌的；灿烂的；极好的；杰出的

etymology.story: 1620s, "marked by grandeur," probably a shortening of earlier splendidious (early 15c.), from Latin splendidus "bright, shining, glittering; sumptuous, gorgeous, grand; illustrious, distinguished, noble; showy, fine, specious," from splendere "be bright, shine, gleam, glisten," from PIE *splend- "to shine, glow" (source also of Lithuanian splendziu "I shine," Middle Irish lainn "bright").

meanings.definitionCn: 回答者， 响应者； 调查对象； 被告

etymology.story: 1520s, "one who answers," from Latin respondentem (nominative respondens), present participle of respondere (see respond)..

meanings.definitionCn: 插曲；一段情节；插话；有趣的事件

etymology.story: 1670s, "commentary between two choric songs in a Greek tragedy," also "an incidental narrative or digression within a story, poem, etc.," from French épisode or directly from Greek epeisodion "an episode," literally "an addition," noun use of neuter of epeisodios "coming in besides," from epi "in addition" (see epi-) + eisodos "a coming in, entrance" (from eis "into" + hodos "way").
late 14c., "agreement, conformity," from Latin convenientia "meeting together, agreement, harmony," from conveniens, present participle of convenire (see convene).

mid-14c., "decision, sentence," from Old French déterminacion (14c.) "determination, settlement, definition," from Latin determinationem (nominative determinatio) "conclusion, boundary," noun of action from past participle stem of determinare "to enclose, bound, set limits to" (see determine).

morphology.mnemonic: age年龄，寿命，时代，时期 + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: late 14c., from evident + -ly (2)..

morphology.mnemonic: 对/向 + 投/掷 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "do an injustice to, dishonor," probably a back-formation from injury, or else from Old French injuriier "to damage; offend," from Latin iniuriari "do an injury," from iniuria.

1660s, "thrust forth, push out," from Latin exertus/exsertus, past participle of exerere/exserere "thrust out, put forth," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + serere "attach, join; arrange, line up" (see series).

early 14c., "country, territory, region," from Old French province "province, part of a country; administrative region for friars" (13c.) and directly from Latin provincia "territory outside Italy under Roman domination," also "a public office; public duty," of uncertain origin, usually explained as pro- "before" + vincere "to conquer" (see victor); but this does not suit the earliest Latin usages.

meanings.definitionCn: 丑闻；流言蜚语；诽谤；公愤

etymology.story: 1580s, "discredit caused by irreligious conduct," from Middle French scandale (12c.), from Late Latin scandalum "cause for offense, stumbling block, temptation," from Greek skandalon "a trap or snare laid for an enemy," in New Testament, metaphorically as "a stumbling block, offense;" originally "trap with a springing device," from PIE *skand- "to leap, climb" (see scan (v.); also see slander (n.), which is another form of the same word).
1530s, "appraise the worth of," from Latin aestimatus, past participle of aestimare "to value, appraise" (see esteem (v.)).
late 14c., from strength + -en (1).

meanings.definitionCn: 水滴，滴水声；静脉滴注；使人厌烦的人

morphology.mnemonic: 出/向外/前任 + 拥有/保持

etymology.story: 1620s, "document or object produced as evidence in court," from Latin exhibitum, noun use of neuter past participle of exhibere "to display, show" (see exhibition).

窒息；噎；[动力] 阻气门
