meanings.definitionCn: 不可避免的,必然发生的

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + age年龄，寿命，时代，时期

etymology.story: "unavoidable," mid-15c., from Latin inevitabilis "unavoidable," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + evitabilis "avoidable," from evitare "to avoid," from ex- "out" (see ex-) + vitare "shun," originally "go out of the way." As a noun from 1850.

meanings.definitionCn: 否定，否认；拒绝给予；拒绝…的要求

etymology.story: early 14c., from Old French denoiir "deny, repudiate, withhold," from Latin denegare "to deny, reject, refuse" (source of Italian dinegarre, Spanish denegar), from de- "away" (see de-) + negare "refuse, say 'no,' " from Old Latin nec "not," from Italic base *nek- "not," from PIE root *ne- "no, not" (see un-).

early 15c., "full of misery, causing wretchedness" (of conditions), from Old French miserable "prone to pity, merciful," and directly from Latin miserabilis "pitiable, miserable, deplorable, lamentable," from miserari "to pity, lament, deplore," from miser "wretched" (see miser).

late 14c., "refreshment or curing of a person, refreshment by eating," from Old French recreacion (13c.), from Latin recreationem (nominative recreatio) "recovery from illness," noun of action from past participle stem of recreare "to refresh, restore, make anew, revive, invigorate," from re- "again" (see re-) + creare (see create).

meanings.definitionCn: 补贴，津贴；零用钱；减价，折扣；允许

etymology.story: late 14c., "praise" (a sense now obsolete), from Old French aloance "allowance, granting, allocation," from alouer (see allow).

meanings.definitionCn: 轻便的,手提(式)的,可移动的

etymology.story: early 15c., from French portable "that can be carried," from Late Latin portabilis "that can be carried," from Latin portare "to carry" (see port (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 古代的；古老的，过时的；年老的

etymology.story: "standard-bearer," 1550s, a corruption of ensign.

mid-14c., "to rejoice" (now always with in), from Old French gloriier "glorify; pride oneself on, boast about," and directly from Latin gloriari which in classical use meant "to boast, vaunt, brag, pride oneself," from gloria (see glory (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 次要的,二级的;中级的,第二的

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin secundarius "pertaining to the second class, inferior," from secundus (see second (adj.)).

1590s, "a sending abroad," originally of Jesuits, from Latin missionem (nominative missio) "act of sending, a despatching; a release, a setting at liberty; discharge from service, dismissal," noun of action from past participle stem of mittere "to send," oldest form probably *smittere, of unknown origin.

Old English wandrian "move about aimlessly, wander," from West Germanic *wandran "to roam about" (source also of Old Frisian wondria, Middle Low German, Middle Dutch wanderen, German wandern "to wander," a variant form of the root represented in Old High German wantalon "to walk, wander"), from PIE root *wendh- "to turn, wind, weave" (see wind (v.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 采取；接受；收养；正式通过
反对,使对立,使对抗,使相对

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French oposer "oppose, resist, rival; contradict, state opposing point of view" (12c.), from poser "to place, lay down" (see pose (v.1)), blended with Latin opponere "oppose, object to, set against" (see opponent).

meanings.definitionCn: 单数的；单一的；非凡的；异常的

morphology.mnemonic: one-base hit, + 与…有关的/像…的

meanings.definitionCn: 装置,设备,仪表;方法,设计

etymology.story: late 13c., from Old French devis "division, separation, disposition, wish, desire; coat of arms, emblem; last will," from deviser "to divide, distribute" (see devise).
"central processor of a computer system," 1964, from main (adj.) + frame (n.)..

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 保持/服务 + 有…倾向的

etymology.story: late 14c., conservatyf, from Middle French conservatif, from Late Latin conservativus, from Latin conservatus, past participle of conservare (see conserve).

meanings.definitionCn: 进入；接入；到达；享用权；入口

etymology.story: 1550s, "act of conferring," from Middle French conférence (15c.), from Medieval Latin conferentia, from Latin conferens, present participle of conferre (see confer).

morphology.mnemonic: 做/驱动 + 有…倾向的 + ity

etymology.story: 1640s, from advise (v.) + -able..
mid-15c., from Latin dormitorium "sleeping place," from dormire "to sleep" (see dormant).
Old English ofercuman "to reach, overtake," also "to conquer, prevail over," from ofer (see over) + cuman "to come" (see come (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 工作 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French cabane "hut, cabin," from Old Provençal cabana, from Late Latin capanna "hut" (source of Spanish cabana, Italian capanna), of doubtful origin.

meanings.definitionCn: 总数， 和；金额；算术题

etymology.story: early 14c., "to count, count up, calculate, reckon," from Old French sommer "to count, add up," or directly from Medieval Latin summare, from summa (see sum (n.)).
late 14c., from Middle French corant (Modern French courant), from Old French corant (see current (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 多样；种类；杂耍；变化，多样化

etymology.story: 1530s, "change of fortunes," from Middle French variété and directly from Latin varietatem (nominative varietas) "difference, diversity; a kind, variety, species, sort," from varius "various" (see vary).

morphology.mnemonic: 通过/彻底 + 站立/设置

etymology.story: 1530s, from Middle French persister (14c.), from Latin persistere "abide, continue steadfastly," from per- "thoroughly" (see per) + sistere "come to stand, cause to stand still" (see assist (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 声音的；[声] 音频的，[声] 声频的

etymology.story: "sound," especially recorded or transmitted, 1934, abstracted from prefix audio- (in audio-frequency, 1919, etc.), from Latin audire "hear" (see audience)..

late 14c., "judge or authority on civil law," from Old French civilien "of the civil law," created from Latin civilis "relating to a citizen, relating to public life, befitting a citizen; popular, affable, courteous" (see civil).

meanings.definitionCn: 刺穿；洞察；响彻；深深地打动

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French à part "to the side," from Latin ad "to" (see ad-) + partem, accusative of pars "a side" (see part (n.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 思索；推测vt.投机；思索，推测

etymology.story: 1590s, "view mentally, contemplate" (transitive), back-formation from speculation.

发射；发行，投放市场；下水；汽艇

etymology.story: late 14c., from past participle of verb belove (c.
"to separate from the herd" (originally in deer-hunting, often with forth or out), 1570s, from single (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 确认；确定；证实；批准；使巩固

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 坚固/稳定

etymology.story: mid-13c., confirmyn "to ratify," from Old French confermer (13c., Modern French confirmer) "strengthen, establish, consolidate; affirm by proof or evidence; anoint (a king)," from Latin confirmare "make firm, strengthen, establish," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + firmare "to strengthen," from firmus "strong, steadfast" (see firm (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 巩固，加强；用水泥涂；接合

etymology.story: 1825, "underground passage" (for water pipes or pedestrians, later for electrical wires), from sub- + way (n.).

late 14c., "to decide, resolve; to arrange the time of (a meeting, etc.)," from Anglo-French appointer, Old French apointier "make ready, arrange, settle, place" (12c.), from apointer "duly, fitly," from phrase à point "to the point," from a- "to" (see ad-) + point "point," from Latin punctum (see point (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 有弹性的；灵活的；易伸缩的

etymology.story: 1650s, formerly also elastick, coined in French (1650s) as a scientific term to describe gases, from Modern Latin elasticus, from Greek elastos "ductile, flexible," related to elaunein "to strike, beat out," which is of uncertain origin; according to Watkins from an extended form of the PIE base *ele- "to go." Applied to solids from 1670s.

meanings.definitionCn: 广阔的；巨大的；大量的；巨额的

etymology.story: 1570s, "being of great extent or size," from Middle French vaste, from Latin vastus "immense, extensive, huge," also "desolate, unoccupied, empty." The two meanings probably originally attached to two separate words, one with a long -a- one with a short -a-, that merged in early Latin (see waste (v.)).

1580s, "intercept" (obsolete), a back-formation from intervention, or else from Latin intervenire "to come between, intervene; interrupt; stand in the way, oppose, hinder," from inter "between" (see inter-) + venire "to come" (see venue).

morphology.mnemonic: 不足/在…之下 + step，go，grade步，走，级

etymology.story: 1620s, a hybrid formed from under + graduate (n.).

early 15c., "creed, summary, religious belief," from Late Latin symbolum "creed, token, mark," from Greek symbolon "token, watchword, sign by which one infers; ticket, a permit, license" (the word was applied c.250 by Cyprian of Carthage to the Apostles' Creed, on the notion of the "mark" that distinguishes Christians from pagans), literally "that which is thrown or cast together," from assimilated form of syn- "together" (see syn-) + bole "a throwing, a casting, the stroke of a missile, bolt, beam," from bol-, nominative stem of ballein "to throw" (see ballistics).

meanings.definitionCn: 商业的；商务的；可获利的

连接，贴合；接合；使有接头

root.meaning: unite, become associated, form an alliance

etymology.story: early 15c., "united or sharing" (in some activity), from Old French jointiz (adj.) "joined together, close together" and Old French joint (14c.), past participle adjective from joindre "to join, connect, unite" (see join (v.))..

meanings.definitionCn: 合理的，公道的；通情达理的
可用的， 可得到的；可以见到的， 随时可来的

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 强壮/价值 + able

morphology.mnemonic: 向/朝/在 + 呼叫/声音 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1550s, "state of being crowded" (now obsolete); 1640s, "fact of occurring often;" from Latin frequentia "an assembling in great numbers, a crowding; crowd, multitude, throng," from frequentem (see frequent).

1590s, from lug (v.) "to drag" + -age; so, literally "what has to be lugged about" (or, in Johnson's definition, "any thing of more weight than value").

meanings.definitionCn: 导弹的；可投掷的；用以发射导弹的

令人满意的,圆满的,良好的,符合要求的

etymology.story: mid-15c., "capable of atoning for sin," from Middle French satisfactoire (14c.) and directly from Late Latin satisfactorius, from Latin satisfactus, past participle of satisfacere (see satisfy).

meanings.definitionCn: 军事的；军人的；适于战争的
不情愿的；勉强的；顽抗的

etymology.story: "unwilling," 1660s, from Latin reluctantem (nominative reluctans), present participle of reluctari (see reluctance).

meanings.definitionCn: 不寻常的；与众不同的；不平常的

etymology.story: 1580s, from un- (1) "not" + usual (adj.).

meanings.definitionCn: 部门；扇形，扇区；象限仪；函数尺

etymology.story: 1560s, "section of a circle between two radii," from Late Latin sector "section of a circle," in classical Latin "a cutter, one who cuts," from sectus, past participle of secare "to cut" (see section (n.)).

late 12c., "sufferings of Christ on the Cross," from Old French passion "Christ's passion, physical suffering" (10c.), from Late Latin passionem (nominative passio) "suffering, enduring," from past participle stem of Latin pati "to suffer, endure," possibly from PIE root *pe(i)- "to hurt" (see fiend).

meanings.definitionCn: 极端的；极度的；偏激的；尽头的
极端；末端；最大程度；极端的事物

etymology.story: late 14c., from Anglo-French enseurer, from en- "make" (see en- (1)) + Old French seur "sure" (see sure); probably influenced by Old French asseurer "assure."

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + 顺序/等级 + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1660s, "to place in the same rank," from Latin coordinare (see coordination).

morphology.mnemonic: musical instrument, + 主义/学说/运动

meanings.definitionCn: 传染病；流行病；风尚等的流行
料想,猜想;假定;conj.假使...结果会怎样

etymology.story: early 14c., "to assume as the basis of argument," from Old French suposer "to assume" (13c.), probably a replacement (influenced by Old French poser "put, place") of *suppondre, from Latin supponere "put or place under; to subordinate, make subject," from sub "under" (see sub-) + ponere "put, place" (past participle positus; see position (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 正直的，诚实的；垂直的，直立的；笔直的；合乎正道的

etymology.story: Old English upriht "erect, face-upward;" see up (adv.) + right (adj.1).

meanings.definitionCn: 值得注意的;显著的,异常的,非凡的
使成管状；把…装管；用管输送

etymology.story: 1610s, from Middle French tube (15c.), from Latin tubus "tube, pipe," of unknown origin.

morphology.mnemonic: 船 + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., from Old French naval (14c.) and directly from Latin navalis "pertaining to a ship or ships," from navis "ship," from PIE *nau- (2) "boat" (source also of Sanskrit nauh, accusative navam "ship, boat;" Armenian nav "ship;" Greek naus "ship," nautes "sailor;" Old Irish nau "ship;" Welsh noe "a flat vessel;" Old Norse nor "ship").
1640s, failer, "a failing, deficiency," also "act of failing," from Anglo-French failer, Old French falir "be lacking; not succeed" (see fail (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 会计工作， 会计职业

root.meaning: to reckon for money given or received, render a reckoning,

morphology.mnemonic: to reckon for money given or received, render a reckoning, + ance + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1854, from accountant + -cy..

late 14c., "a smithy," from Old French forge "forge, smithy" (12c.), earlier faverge, from Latin fabrica "workshop, smith's shop," hence also "a trade, an industry;" also "a skillful production, a crafty device," from faber (genitive fabri) "workman in hard materials, smith" (see fabric).

late 14c., "to disperse, dissipate, loosen," from Latin solvere "to loosen, dissolve; untie, release, detach; depart; unlock; scatter; dismiss; accomplish, fulfill; explain; remove," from PIE *se-lu-, from reflexive pronoun *s(w)e- (see idiom) + root *leu- "to loosen, divide, cut apart" (see lose).

meanings.definitionCn: （绳等的）结；节瘤，疙瘩；海里/小时（航速单位）

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + animal of vast size + 使…/具有…的

etymology.story: 1550s, "to point out," from Latin demonstratus, past participle of demonstrare "to indicate, describe" (see demonstration).

morphology.mnemonic: to do, make. + 以…方式/副词化

etymology.story: Old English gearlic "yearly, of the year, annual;" see year + -ly (1)..

morphology.mnemonic: 向下/移除/取消 + 拿/领会

meanings.definitionCn: 茶托，浅碟；浅碟形物；眼睛

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Anglo-Latin saucerium and Old French saussier (Modern French saucière) "sauce dish," from Late Latin salsarium, neuter of salsarius "of or for salted things," from Latin salsus (see sauce (n.)).
"payment for work, use, or services; wages," from late Old English hyr "wages; interest, usury," from the verb or from a Proto-Germanic *hurja- (see hire (v.)).
1785, originally slang, perhaps a diminutive from dun "dull gray-brown," the form perhaps influenced by monkey.

1590s, "act of appointing," from Latin destinationem (nominative destinatio) "purpose, design," from past participle stem of destinare "determine, appoint, choose, make firm or fast," from de- "completely, formally" (see de-) + -stinare (related to stare "to stand") from PIE *ste-no-, from root *sta- "to stand" (see stet).
1550s, "of or at the vertex, directly overhead," from Middle French vertical (1540s), from Late Latin verticalis "overhead," from Latin vertex (genitive verticis) "highest point" (see vertex).
Old English leornung "study, action of acquiring knowledge," verbal noun from leornian (see learn).

meanings.definitionCn: 纪念碑,纪念馆,遗迹,不朽的业绩

etymology.story: late 13c., "a sepulchre," from Old French monument "grave, tomb, monument," and directly from Latin monumentum "a monument, memorial structure, statue; votive offering; tomb; memorial record," literally "something that reminds," from monere "to remind, warn" (see monitor (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 错误地/不当地 + 共同/联合 + 取/抓 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: 1660s, from mis- (1) + conception.

鲜艳的;生动的,栩栩如生的

etymology.story: 1630s, from French vivide and perhaps also directly from Latin vividus "spirited, animated, lively, full of life," from vivus "alive," from PIE *gweie- (1) "to live" (see bio-).

meanings.definitionCn: 对…说甜言蜜语；加蜜使甜

etymology.story: "not present, absent," 1520s, from present participle of miss (v.).
1765, from emphasis + -ize.

socio + 与…有关的/形容词 + …主义者/…专家

etymology.story: "one who advocates socialism," 1827, from French socialiste, or else a native formation based on it, in reference to the teachings of Comte de Saint-Simon, founder of French socialism.
early 15c., "having steps or ridges," from Medieval Latin gradualis, from Latin gradus "step" (see grade (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 数字；人物；图形；价格；（人的）体形；画像

etymology.story: late 14c., "to represent" (in painting or sculpture), "make a likeness," also "to have a certain shape or appearance," from Old French figurer, from Latin figurare (see figure (n.)).

12c., a merger of Old English ora "ore, unworked metal" (related to eorþe "earth," see earth; and cognate with Low German ur "iron-containing ore," Dutch oer, Old Norse aurr "gravel"); and Old English ar "brass, copper, bronze," from Proto-Germanic *ajiz- (source also of Old Norse eir "brass, copper," German ehern "brazen," Gothic aiz "bronze"), from PIE root *aus- (2) "gold" (see aureate).

meanings.definitionCn: 轻微的，少量的；脆弱的；细长的；不重要的

etymology.story: 1620s, from Latin praevius "going before," from prae "before" (see pre-) + via "road" (see via).

meanings.definitionCn: 传输；传播；发射；传达；遗传

morphology.mnemonic: socio + 与…有关的/形容词 + 主义/学说/运动

etymology.story: 1837, from French socialisme (1832) or formed in English (based on socialist) from social (adj.) + -ism.

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/联合 + sume + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "wasting of the body by disease; wasting disease" (replacing Old English yfeladl "the evil disease"), from Old French consumpcion, from Latin consumptionem (nominative consumptio) "a using up, wasting," noun of state from past participle stem of consumere (see consume).

meanings.definitionCn: 科学技术；工业技术；应用科学

morphology.mnemonic: 技术 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: 1610s, "a discourse or treatise on an art or the arts," from Greek tekhnologia "systematic treatment of an art, craft, or technique," originally referring to grammar, from tekhno- (see techno-) + -logy.
late 14c., "to become feeble," from weak + -en (1).

late 13c., "to condemn," from Old French damner "damn, condemn; convict, blame; injure," derivative of Latin damnare "to adjudge guilty; to doom; to condemn, blame, reject," from noun damnum "damage, hurt, harm; loss, injury; a fine, penalty," from Proto-Italic *dapno-, possibly from an ancient religious term from PIE *dap- "to apportion in exchange" [see Watkins].

meanings.definitionCn: 上级的；优秀的，出众的；高傲的
上级，长官；优胜者，高手；长者

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin superior (see superior (adj.)), used in Medieval Latin with a noun sense of "one higher, a superior.".

morphology.mnemonic: 地方 + 使…/具有…的 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: "position, place," 1590s, from Latin locationem (nominative locatio), noun of action from past participle stem of locare (see locate); Hollywood sense of "place outside a film studio where a scene is filmed" is from 1914..

meanings.definitionCn: 补偿(或赔偿)的款物；补偿，赔偿

morphology.mnemonic: to recompense, remunerate + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "action of compensating," from Latin compensationem (nominative compensatio) "a weighing one thing against another, a balancing," noun of action from past participle stem of compensare (see compensate).

early 15c., "to take notice of," from Middle French advertiss-, present participle stem of a(d)vertir "to warn" (12c.), from Latin advertere "turn toward," from ad- "toward" (see ad-) + vertere "to turn" (see versus).
1851, soft wares, "woolen or cotton fabrics," also, "relatively perishable consumer goods," from soft + ware (n.).
1887, "special group-study class for advanced students," from German Seminar "group of students working with a professor," from Latin seminarium "breeding ground, plant nursery" (see seminary).
mid-15c., from Middle French comparatif, from Latin comparativus "pertaining to comparison," from comparat-, past participle stem of comparare (see comparison).

meanings.definitionCn: 竞争，比赛；角逐，较量；竞争者

morphology.mnemonic: 竞争 + 状态/行为/过程

反应,起作用;(against)反对,起反作用

etymology.story: 1640s, "to exert, as a thing acted upon, an opposite action upon the agent," from re- + act (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 龙；凶暴的人，凶恶的人；严厉而有警觉性的女人

etymology.story:
early 13c., from Old French dragon, from Latin draconem (nominative draco) "huge serpent, dragon," from Greek drakon (genitive drakontos) "serpent, giant seafish," apparently from drak-, strong aorist stem of derkesthai "to see clearly," from PIE *derk- "to see." Perhaps the literal sense is "the one with the (deadly) glance."

The young are dragonets (14c.).

meanings.definitionCn: 迟疑的；踌躇的；犹豫不定的

etymology.story: 1640s, probably a back-formation from hesitancy, or else from Latin haesitantem.

meanings.definitionCn: 在…较远的一边； 超出， 除…之外

该死；见鬼（表示惊奇、烦恼、厌恶、恼怒、失望等）

etymology.story: also Hell, Old English hel, helle, "nether world, abode of the dead, infernal regions, place of torment for the wicked after death," from Proto-Germanic *haljo "the underworld" (source also of Old Frisian helle, Old Saxon hellia, Dutch hel, Old Norse hel, German Hölle, Gothic halja "hell").
1920, in the electronics sense, "the return of a fraction of an output signal to the input of an earlier stage," from verbal phrase, from feed (v.) + back (adv.).

meanings.definitionCn: 赌运气；冒…的危险，使遭受危险

etymology.story: "put something at stake in a game of chance," 1520s, from Middle French hasarder "to play at gambling, throw dice" (15c.), from hasard (see hazard (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 意志，意愿 + …的场所/与…有关

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin voluntarius "willing, of one's free will," from voluntas "will," from the ancient accusative singular present participle of velle "to wish" (see will (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: to establish a relationship + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: Middle English conneccion (late 14c.), also connexioun (mid-15c.), from Old French connexion, from Latin connexionem (nominative connexio) "a binding or joining together," from *connexare, frequentative of conectere "to fasten together, to tie, join together," from com- "together" (see com-) + nectere "to bind, tie" (see nexus).

Old English timber "building, structure," in late Old English "building material, trees suitable for building," and "trees or woods in general," from Proto-Germanic *timran (source also of Old Saxon timbar "a building, room," Old Frisian timber "wood, building," Old High German zimbar "timber, wooden dwelling, room," Old Norse timbr "timber," German Zimmer "room"), from PIE *deme- "to build," possibly from root *dem- "house, household" (source of Greek domos, Latin domus; see domestic (adj.)).
mid-14c., "fact of being present," from Old French presence (12c., Modern French présence), from Latin praesentia "a being present," from praesentem (see present (n.)).
1590s, "power to accomplish something," from Latin efficientia "efficient power; efficiency; influence" (from efficientem; see efficient) + -cy.

meanings.definitionCn: 在头顶上的； 架空的 A

etymology.story: 1530s, "above one's head" (adv.), from over- + head.

early 14c., from Old French contract (Modern French contrat), from Latin contractus "a contract, agreement," from past participle of contrahere "to draw together," metaphorically, "to make a bargain," from com- "together" (see com-) + trahere "to draw" (see tract (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 结束,终止;断定,下结论;缔结,议定

morphology.mnemonic: 战争 + 与…有关的/像…的

meanings.definitionCn: 革新的， 创新的， 新颖的； 富有革新精神的

root.meaning: bring in new things, alter established practices

morphology.mnemonic: 再/重新/回 + volve + 状态/行为/过程 + …的场所/与…有关

mid-15c., from Latin exactus, past participle of exigere "require, enforce, demand, collect (money);" see exact (adj.).
also in-door, 1711, opposed to outdoor, contracted from within door; the form indoors is attested from 1759 (within-doors is from 1750); as an adverb from 1801..

morphology.mnemonic: 共同/一起 + 感情/疾病 + ic

etymology.story: 1640s, "pertaining to sympathy," from Modern Latin sympatheticus, from late Greek sympathetikos "having sympathy," from sympathein, from sympathes "having a fellow feeling, affected by like feelings" (see sympathy).
"to load (a ship) with goods or merchandise for shipment," mid-15c.

meanings.definitionCn: （在内）变动；平行，列为一行；延伸；漫游；射程达到
分发， 分配； 配给物； 散布， 分布

morphology.mnemonic: 不/分离 + 给予/分配 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: mid-14c., from Old French distribution (13c.) and directly from Latin distributionem (nominative distributio) "a division, distribution," noun of action from past participle stem of distribuere "deal out in portions," from dis- "individually" + tribuere "assign, allot" (see tribute)..
variant spelling of tire (n.), chiefly British English..

late 14c., from Old French deriver "to flow, pour out; derive, originate," from Latin derivare "to lead or draw off (a stream of water) from its source" (in Late Latin also "to derive"), from phrase de rivo (de "from" + rivus "stream;" see rivulet).

late 14c., "come to blows with," from Old French couper, earlier colper "hit, punch," from colp "a blow" (see coup).

逐渐变平；[音乐]以降调唱（或奏）

etymology.story: also water-proof, 1725, from water (n.1) + proof (n.).

mid-15c., from Middle French précis "condensed, cut short" (14c.) and directly from Medieval Latin precisus, from Latin praecisus "abrupt, abridged, cut off," past participle of praecidere "to cut off, shorten," from prae "before" (see pre-) + caedere "to cut" (see -cide; for Latin vowel change, see acquisition).

meanings.definitionCn: 明智的,达理的;可觉察的,明显的

root.meaning: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning

morphology.mnemonic: be conscious inwardly of (one's state or condition) is from 1680s. Meaning  + able

etymology.story: late 14c., "capable of sensation or feeling;" also "capable of being sensed or felt, perceptible to the senses," hence "easily understood; logical, reasonable," from Late Latin sensibilis "having feeling: perceptible by the senses," from sensus, past participle of sentire "perceive, feel" (see sense (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: (to)反对,异议,不喜欢,反对的理由

morphology.mnemonic: 对/向 + 投/掷 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., from Old French objeccion "reply, retort" (12c.) and directly from Late Latin obiectionem (nominative obiectio), "a throwing or putting before," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin obicere "to oppose" (see object (n.))..

root.meaning: to attend to in practice, to keep, follow

morphology.mnemonic: to attend to in practice, to keep, follow + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., "performance of a religious rite," from Latin observationem (nominative observatio) "a watching over, observance, investigation," noun of action from past participle stem of observare (see observe).

meanings.definitionCn: 道歉， 谢罪， 认错

etymology.story: 1590s, "to speak in defense of;" see apology + -ize.

morphology.mnemonic: in + dwell居住 + …的人/处于…状态

etymology.story: "one who dwells in a place" (as distinguished from a visitor or transient), early 15c., from Anglo-French inhabitant, from Latin inhabitantem (nominative inhabitans), present participle of inhabitare "to dwell in" (see inhabit).

meanings.definitionCn: 碎片,小部分,一点儿;分数

morphology.mnemonic: 打破 + 状态/行为/过程

etymology.story: late 14c., originally in the mathematical sense, from Anglo-French fraccioun (Old French fraccion, "a breaking," 12c., Modern French fraction) and directly from Late Latin fractionem (nominative fractio) "a breaking," especially into pieces, in Medieval Latin "a fragment, portion," noun of action from past participle stem of Latin frangere "to break (something) in pieces, shatter, fracture," from Proto-Italic *frang-, from a nasalized variant of PIE root *bhreg- "to break" (source also of Sanskrit (giri)-bhraj "breaking-forth (out of the mountains);" Gothic brikan, Old English brecan "to break;" Lithuanian brasketi "crash, crack;" Old Irish braigim "break wind").

meanings.definitionCn: 滤波器；[化工] 过滤器；筛选；滤光器

etymology.story: 1570s (transitive), from French filtrer or from Medieval Latin filtrare, from filtrum "felt" (see filter (n.)).

1570s, "a (social) moving, stirring, agitation," from Middle French émotion (16c.), from Old French emouvoir "stir up" (12c.), from Latin emovere "move out, remove, agitate," from assimilated form of ex- "out" (see ex-) + movere "to move" (see move (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 大量的；巨大的，厚重的；魁伟的

etymology.story: early 15c., "to fix the amount (of a tax, fine, etc.)," from Anglo-French assesser, from Medieval Latin assessare "fix a tax upon," originally frequentative of Latin assessus "a sitting by," past participle of assidere "to sit beside" (and thus to assist in the office of a judge), from ad- "to" (see ad-) + sedere "to sit" (see sedentary).

meanings.definitionCn: 资产， 财产；优点； 有用的东西
欠；感激；应给予；应该把……归功于

etymology.story: Old English agan (past tense ahte) "to have, own," from Proto-Germanic *aigan "to possess" (source also of Old Frisian aga, Old Norse eiga, Old High German eigan, Gothic aigan "to possess, have"), from PIE *aik- "to be master of, possess" (source also of Sanskrit ise "he owns," isah "owner, lord, ruler;" Avestan is- "riches," isvan- "well-off, rich").

meanings.definitionCn: 最小值；最低限度；最小化；最小量
数学的，数学上的；精确的

morphology.mnemonic: that which is learnt; + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 15c., from Latin mathematicus (see mathematic) + -al (1).

morphology.mnemonic: 统治 + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: early 15c., "defense, justification," from Late Latin apologia, from Greek apologia "a speech in defense," from apologeisthai "to speak in one's defense," from apologos "an account, story," from apo- "from, off" (see apo-) + logos "speech" (see lecture (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 不可思议的；有魔力的；魔术的
论证；论据；争吵；内容提要

morphology.mnemonic: to oppose, dispute + 结果/过程/状态

etymology.story: early 14c., "statements and reasoning in support of a proposition," from Old French arguement "reasoning, opinion; accusation, charge" (13c.), from Latin argumentum "evidence, ground, support, proof; a logical argument," from arguere "to argue" (see argue).

late 14c., from Old French reveler "reveal" (14c.), from Latin revelare "reveal, uncover, disclose," literally "unveil," from re- "opposite of" (see re-) + velare "to cover, veil," from velum "a veil" (see veil (n.)).
1640s, plural of datum, from Latin datum "(thing) given," neuter past participle of dare "to give" (see date (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 理论的；理论上的；假设的；推理的

morphology.mnemonic: 神 + ic + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: 1610s, "contemplative," with -al (1) + Late Latin theoreticus "of or pertaining to theory," from Greek theoretikos "contemplative, speculative, pertaining to theory" (by Aristotle contrasted to praktikos), from theoretos "that may be seen or considered," from theorein "to consider, look at" (see theory).

meanings.definitionCn: 充足的，足够的；适当的，胜任的

etymology.story: 1610s, from Latin adaequatus "equalized," past participle of adaequare "to make equal to," from ad- "to" (see ad-) + aequare "make level," from aequus (see equal (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 完全的；彻底的；无条件的

etymology.story: early 12c., from Anglo-French union, Old French oignon "onion" (formerly also oingnon), and directly from Latin unionem (nominative unio), colloquial rustic Roman for "a kind of onion," also "pearl" (via notion of a string of onions), literally "one, unity;" sense connection is the successive layers of an onion, in contrast with garlic or cloves.

1610s (intransitive) "move to and fro;" 1660s, "swing to and fro;" from Latin vibratus, past participle of vibrare "set in tremulous motion, move quickly to and fro, quiver, tremble, shake," from PIE *wib-ro-, from root *weip- "to turn, vacillate, tremble ecstatically, move quickly to and fro" (source also of Lithuanian wyburiu "to wag" (the tail), Danish vippe, Dutch wippen "to swing," Old English wipan "to wipe").

meanings.definitionCn: 香肠；腊肠；装香肠的碎肉

etymology.story: mid-15c., sawsyge, from Old North French saussiche (Modern French saucisse), from Vulgar Latin *salsica "sausage," from salsicus "seasoned with salt," from Latin salsus "salted" (see sauce)..

meanings.definitionCn: 极大的，巨大的；惊人的；极好的

etymology.story: 1630s, "awful, dreadful, terrible," from Latin tremendus "fearful, to be dreaded, terrible," literally "to be trembled at," gerundive form of tremere "to tremble" (see tremble (v.)).

early 15c., from Middle French condenser (14c.) or directly from Latin condensare "to make dense," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + densare "make thick," from densus "dense, thick, crowded," a word used of crowds, darkness, clouds, etc.
early 14c., barere, from Anglo-French barrere, Old French barriere "obstacle, gatekeeper," from barre "bar" (see bar (n.1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 现实的；现实主义的；逼真的；实在论的

etymology.story: "true to reality" (of art, literature, etc.), 1829; "involving a practical view of life" (opposed to idealistic), 1831; from realist + -ic.

meanings.definitionCn: 司法，法律制裁；正义；法官，审判员

etymology.story: mid-12c., "the exercise of authority in vindication of right by assigning reward or punishment;" also "quality of being fair and just; moral soundness and conformity to truth," from Old French justice "justice, legal rights, jurisdiction" (11c.), from Latin iustitia "righteousness, equity," from iustus "upright, just" (see just (adj.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 创造,创作;引起,造成,建立

etymology.story: late 14c., from Latin creatus, past participle of creare "to make, bring forth, produce, beget," related to crescere "arise, grow" (see crescent).

morphology.mnemonic: 判定/控告 + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 标签；名称；结束语；附属物
尾随，紧随；连接；起浑名；添饰

etymology.story: 1570s, from note + book (n.)..

meanings.definitionCn: 好奇的, 求知的, 古怪的, 爱挑剔的

etymology.story: mid-14c., "eager to know" (often in a bad sense), from Old French curios "solicitous, anxious, inquisitive; odd, strange" (Modern French curieux) and directly from Latin curiosus "careful, diligent; inquiring eagerly, meddlesome," akin to cura "care" (see cure (n.)).
late 14c., from Old French indirect (14c.) or directly from Late Latin indirectus "not direct," from in- "not, opposite of" (see in- (1)) + directus (see direct (adj.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 不/进入 + watershed, separation between river valleys + 与…有关的/形容词

meanings.definitionCn: 丑陋的；邪恶的；令人厌恶的

etymology.story: mid-13c., uglike "frightful or horrible in appearance," from a Scandinavian source, such as Old Norse uggligr "dreadful, fearful," from uggr "fear, apprehension, dread" (perhaps related to agg "strife, hate") + -ligr "-like" (see -ly (1)).

meanings.definitionCn: 讨厌的人；损害；麻烦事；讨厌的东西

etymology.story: early 14c., "obligatory contribution levied by a sovereign or government," from Anglo-French tax, Old French taxe, and directly from Medieval Latin taxa, from Latin taxare (see tax (v.)).
late 13c., eorthequakynge, from earth + quake (n.).

meanings.definitionCn: 兴奋；刺激；令人兴奋的事物

etymology.story: early 15c., "encouragement;" c.

meanings.definitionCn: 恶梦；可怕的事物，无法摆脱的恐惧

etymology.story: late 13c., "an evil female spirit afflicting sleepers with a feeling of suffocation," compounded from night + mare (3) "goblin that causes nightmares, incubus." Meaning shifted mid-16c.
"a coming to an end," early 14c., verbal noun from end (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 核心；要点；果心；[计] 磁心
委员会；会议；理事会；地方议会；顾问班子

etymology.story: early 12c., from Anglo-French cuncile, from Old North French concilie (Old French concile, 12c.) "assembly; council meeting; body of counsellors," from Latin concilium "a meeting, a gathering of people," from PIE *kal-yo-, from root *kele- (2) "to shout" (see claim (v.)).

morphology.mnemonic: 使成为/放入 + bring about by unusual effort

etymology.story: mid-14c., "to drive by physical force; to try, attempt, strive; to fortify, strengthen a place;" late 14c.

1570s, "position of an ambassador," from Middle French embassee "mission, charge, office of ambassador," Old French ambassee, from Italian ambasciata, from Old Provençal ambaisada "office of ambassador," from Gaulish *ambactos "dependant, vassal," literally "one going around," from PIE *amb(i)-ag-to, from *ambi- (see ambi-) + *ambi- "around" (see ambi-) + *ag- "to drive, move" (see act (n.)).

morphology.mnemonic: separate division of a government + 行为/过程/结果

etymology.story: mid-15c., from Old French deporteure "departure," figuratively, "death," from departir (see depart) + -ure (see -ure)..

meanings.definitionCn: 具体的,实质性的n.混凝土v.用混凝土修筑

etymology.story: late 14c., "actual, solid," from Latin concretus "condensed, hardened, thick, hard, stiff, curdled, congealed, clotted," figuratively "thick; dim," literally "grown together;" past participle of concrescere "to grow together," from com- "together" (see com-) + crescere "to grow" (see crescent).
mid-15c., from Middle French penalité and directly from Medieval Latin poenalitatem (nominative poenalitas), from Latin poenalis (see penal).

the science of social phenomena, 1842, from French sociologie, a hybrid coined 1830 by French philosopher Isidore Auguste Comte (1798-1857), from Latin socius "associate" (see social (adj.)) + Greek-derived suffix -logie (see -logy)..

Old English fleon, flion "take flight, fly from, avoid, escape" (contracted class II strong verb; past tense fleah, past participle flogen), from Proto-Germanic *fleuhan "to run away" (source also of Old High German fliohan, Old Norse flöja, Old Frisian flia, Dutch vlieden, German fliehen, Gothic þliuhan "to flee"), probably from PIE *pleuk-, extended form of root *pleu- "to flow" (see pluvial).

meanings.definitionCn: 明显的；独特的；清楚的；有区别的

etymology.story: late 14c., originally past participle of distincten (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 刻度;天平,磅秤;比例尺;规模;音阶;鱼鳞

morphology.mnemonic: to regain health or strength + 具有…特征/名词化

etymology.story: mid-14c., "return to health," from Anglo-French recoverie (c.

meanings.definitionCn: 用树篱笆围住；避免作正面答复
对冲，套期保值；树篱；障碍
哭泣；流泪；哀悼；滴落；渗出液体

etymology.story: Old English wepan "shed tears, cry; bewail, mourn over; complain" (class VII strong verb; past tense weop, past participle wopen), from Proto-Germanic *wopjan (source also of Old Norse op, Old High German wuof "shout, shouting, crying," Old Saxon wopian, Gothic wopjan "to shout, cry out, weep"), from PIE *wab- "to cry, scream" (source also of Latin vapulare "to be flogged;" Old Church Slavonic vupiti "to call," vypu "gull").

meanings.definitionCn: 意识到的；知道的；有…方面知识的；懂世故的

etymology.story: late Old English gewær, from Proto-Germanic *ga-waraz (source also of Old Saxon giwar, Middle Dutch gheware, Old High German giwar, German gewahr), from *ga-, intensive prefix, + waraz "wary, cautious" (see wary)..

meanings.definitionCn: 戏剧； 剧本；戏剧性事件或场面

etymology.story: 1510s, from Late Latin drama "play, drama," from Greek drama (genitive dramatos) "play, action, deed," from dran "to do, act, perform" (especially some great deed, whether good or bad), from PIE *dere- "to work." Drama queen attested by 1992..

meanings.definitionCn: 跛行，一拐一拐地走；缓慢费力地前进

etymology.story: 1706, "flaccid, drooping," of obscure origin, perhaps related to limp (v.)..
late 14c., "decision after consideration," from Anglo-French award, Old French esguard, from esguarder (see award (v.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 组织；使有系统化；给予生机；组织成立工会等

etymology.story: early 15c., "construct, establish," from Middle French organiser and directly from Medieval Latin organizare, from Latin organum "instrument, organ" (see organ).

mid-15c., "heated room, bath-room," from Middle Low German or Middle Dutch stove, both meaning "heated room," which was the original sense in English; a general West Germanic word (Old English stofa "bath-room," Old High German stuba, German Stube "sitting room").
"to clear the ground of weeds," late Old English weodian "to weed," from the source of weed (n.).

morphology.mnemonic: to hang together + 与…有关的/形容词

etymology.story: early 14c., continuell, from Old French continuel (12c.), from Latin continuus (see continue).

meanings.definitionCn: 切断…的手足；从…上截下树枝

etymology.story: 1550s, past participle adjective from limit (v.); as a stand-alone for limited express train, by 1883.

meanings.definitionCn: 空闲的， 闲置的；无用的， 无效的；懒散的

morphology.mnemonic: to share a secret with + ance

etymology.story: early 15c., from Middle French confidence or directly from Latin confidentia, from confidentem (nominative confidens) "firmly trusting, bold," present participle of confidere "to have full trust or reliance," from com-, intensive prefix (see com-), + fidere "to trust" (see faith).

meanings.definitionCn: [数] 任意的；武断的；专制的

etymology.story: early 15c., "deciding by one's own discretion," from Old French arbitraire (14c.) or directly from Latin arbitrarius "depending on the will, uncertain," from arbiter (see arbiter).
mid-14c., "causing fear," from fear (n.) + -ful.

meanings.definitionCn: 口音；重音；强调；特点；重音符号

etymology.story: "to pronounce with accent or stress," 1520s, from Middle French accenter, from Old French acenter, from acent (see accent (n.)).

meanings.definitionCn: 被动的，消极的；被动语态的

etymology.story: late 14c., in grammatical sense (opposed to active), Old French passif "suffering, undergoing hardship" (14c.) and directly from Latin passivus "capable of feeling or suffering," from pass-, past participle stem of pati "to suffer" (see passion).

meanings.definitionCn: [肥料] 肥料；受精媒介物；促进发展者

etymology.story: 1660s, "something that fertilizes (land)," agent noun from fertilize.

meanings.definitionCn: （英）卡车；[车辆] 货车；运料车

etymology.story: "a truck; a long, flat wagon," 1838, British railroad word, probably from verb lurry "to pull, tug" (1570s), of uncertain origin.
late 14c., agent noun from carry (v.).

meanings.definitionCn: 相当的，相应的；一致的；通信的

etymology.story: 1570s, past participle adjective from correspond.

late 14c., "treatment, discussion," from Anglo-French treté, Old French traitié "assembly, agreement, dealings," from Latin tractatus "discussion, handling, management," from tractare "to handle, manage" (see treat (v.)).
1620s, from Latin emittere "send forth," from assimilated form of ex- "out" (see ex-) + mittere "to send" (see mission).

meanings.definitionCn: 种类，部属，类目；范畴，类型

etymology.story: 1580s, from Middle French catégorie, from Late Latin categoria, from Greek kategoria "accusation, prediction, category," verbal noun from kategorein "to speak against; to accuse, assert, predicate," from kata "down to" (or perhaps "against;" see cata-) + agoreuein "to harangue, to declaim (in the assembly)," from agora "public assembly" (see agora).

meanings.definitionCn: 想要； 打算， 企图
突然折断，拉断；猛咬；啪地关上
